For the dashboard, Streamlit is chosen for its simplicity and real-time update capabilities. Data fetching will use the official subgraph APIs (GraphQL) for Uniswap, PancakeSwap, and Sushiswap. The system will consist of modular fetchers for each DEX, a token analyzer to compare prices and filter opportunities, and a dashboard component for display. Async programming (asyncio, aiohttp) will be used for efficient real-time updates. Error handling and rate limiting will be built in.\n\n## File list\n\n- main.py\n- dex_fetcher.py\n- token_analyzer.py\n- dashboard.py\n- config.py\n- requirements.txt\n- docs/system_design.md\n\n## Data structures and interfaces:\n\n```mermaid\nclassDiagram\n    class DEXFetcher {\n        +__init__(name: str, endpoint: str)\n        +fetch_tokens() -> dict[str, TokenData]\n    }\n    class TokenData {\n        +name: str\n        +contract_address: str\n        +price: float\n        +exchange: str\n    }\n    class TokenAnalyzer {\n        +__init__(dex_fetchers: list[DEXFetcher])\n        +get_common_tokens() -> dict[str, list[TokenData]]\n        +find_arbitrage_opportunities(threshold: float=1.8) -> list[ArbitrageOpportunity]\n    }\n    class ArbitrageOpportunity {\n        +token_name: str\n        +contract_address: str\n        +lowest_price: float\n        +highest_price: float\n        +lowest_exchange: str\n        +highest_exchange: str\n        +price_diff_percent: float\n    }\n    class Dashboard {\n        +__init__(analyzer: TokenAnalyzer)\n        +render_table(opportunities: list[ArbitrageOpportunity])\n        +run()\n    }\n    DEXFetcher <.. TokenAnalyzer\n    TokenAnalyzer <.. Dashboard\n    TokenData <.. ArbitrageOpportunity\n```\n\n## Program call flow:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant M as main.py\n    participant DFU as DEXFetcher(Uniswap)\n    participant DFP as DEXFetcher(PancakeSwap)\n    participant DFS as DEXFetcher(Sushiswap)\n    participant TA as TokenAnalyzer\n    participant DB as Dashboard\n    M->>DFU: fetch_tokens()\n    M->>DFP: fetch_tokens()\n    M->>DFS: fetch_tokens()\n    DFU-->>M: return tokens\n    DFP-->>M: return tokens\n    DFS-->>M: return tokens\n    M->>TA: get_common_tokens()\n    TA-->>M: return common tokens\n    M->>TA: find_arbitrage_opportunities(threshold=1.8)\n    TA-->>M: return opportunities\n    M->>DB: render_table(opportunities)\n    DB-->>M: display dashboard\n    Note over M,DB: Repeat every second\n```\n\n## Anything UNCLEAR\n\n- Preferred dashboard framework: Streamlit is chosen for now, but Dash or Flask could be considered.\n- Should the dashboard support mobile devices? an existing plan to achieve the goal. A plan consists of one to 3 tasks.\nIf plan is created, you should track the progress and update the plan accordingly, such as Plan.finish_current_task, Plan.append_task, Plan.reset_task, Plan.replace_task, etc.\nWhen presented a current task, tackle the task using the available commands.\nPay close attention to new user message, review the conversation history, use RoleZero.reply_to_human to respond to new user requirement.\nNote:\n1. If you keeping encountering errors, unexpected situation, or you are not sure of proceeding, use RoleZero.ask_human to ask for help.\n2. Carefully review your progress at the current task, if your actions so far has not fulfilled the task instruction, you should continue with current task. Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/real_time_token_monitoring_dashboard/dex_fetcher.py has been successfully created, with content:
import asyncio
import aiohttp
from typing import Dict

class TokenData:
    def __init__(self, name: str, contract_address: str, price: float, exchange: str):
        self.name = name
        self.contract_address = contract_address
        self.price = price
        self.exchange = exchange

class DEXFetcher:
    def __init__(self, name: str, endpoint: str):
        self.name = name
        self.endpoint = endpoint

    async def fetch_tokens(self) -> Dict[str, TokenData]:
        """
        Fetches token data from the DEX GraphQL endpoint.
        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to TokenData.
        """
        # GraphQL queries for each DEX (Uniswap, PancakeSwap, Sushiswap)
        # These queries fetch top tokens by liquidity and their price.
        # For production, you may want to paginate or fetch more tokens.
        query = """
        {
          tokens(first: 100, orderBy: volumeUSD, orderDirection: desc) {
            id
            symbol
            name
            derivedETH
            derivedUSD
          }
        }
        """
        headers = {
            "Content-Type": "application/json"
        }
        payload = {
            "query": query
        }
        tokens = {}
        try:
            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
                async with session.post(self.endpoint, json=payload, headers=headers, timeout=15) as resp:
                    if resp.status != 200:
                        print(f"[{self.name}] Error: Received status {resp.status}")
                        return tokens
                    data = await resp.json()
                    for token in data.get("data", {}).get("tokens", []):
                        contract_address = token.get("id")
                        name = token.get("name") or token.get("symbol") or contract_address
                        # Prefer derivedUSD if available, else fallback to derivedETH (approximate with ETH price)
                        price = None
                        if token.get("derivedUSD") is not None:
                            try:
                                price = float(token["derivedUSD"])
                            except Exception:
                                price = None
                        elif token.get("derivedETH") is not None:
                            # Fallback: Use ETH price from CoinGecko (could cache for efficiency)
                            eth_price = await self._get_eth_price()
                            try:
                                price = float(token["derivedETH"]) * eth_price
                            except Exception:
                                price = None
                        if price is not None:
                            tokens[contract_address] = TokenData(
                                name=name,
                                contract_address=contract_address,
                                price=price,
                                exchange=self.name
                            )
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"[{self.name}] Exception during fetch: {e}")
        return tokens

    async def _get_eth_price(self) -> float:
        """
        Fetches the current ETH price in USD from CoinGecko.
        Used as a fallback if derivedUSD is not available.
        """
        url = "https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=ethereum&vs_currencies=usd"
        try:
            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
                async with session.get(url, timeout=10) as resp:
                    if resp.status != 200:
                        return 0.0
                    data = await resp.json()
                    return float(data.get("ethereum", {}).get("usd", 0.0))
        except Exception:
            return 0.0

# Example DEX endpoints (mainnet)
UNISWAP_V3_ENDPOINT = "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/uniswap/uniswap-v3"
PANCAKESWAP_ENDPOINT = "https://bsc.streamingfast.io/subgraphs/name/pancakeswap/exchange-v2"
SUSHISWAP_ENDPOINT = "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/sushiswap/exchange"

# For testing purposes
if __name__ == "__main__":
    async def main():
        fetchers = [
            DEXFetcher("Uniswap", UNISWAP_V3_ENDPOINT),
            DEXFetcher("PancakeSwap", PANCAKESWAP_ENDPOINT),
            DEXFetcher("Sushiswap", SUSHISWAP_ENDPOINT)
        ]
        results = await asyncio.gather(*(f.fetch_tokens() for f in fetchers))
        for dex, tokens in zip(fetchers, results):
            print(f"--- {dex.name} ---")
            for t in list(tokens.values())[:5]:  # Print top 5 tokens for brevity
                print(f"{t.name} ({t.contract_address}): ${t.price:.4f}")

    asyncio.run(main())

def fetch_tokens(self) -> Dict[str, TokenData]:\n        """\n        Fetches token data from the DEX GraphQL endpoint.\n        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to TokenData.\n        """\n        # GraphQL queries for each DEX (Uniswap, PancakeSwap, Sushiswap)\n        # These queries fetch top tokens by liquidity and their price.\n        # For production, you may want to paginate or fetch more tokens.\n        query = """\n        {\n          tokens(first: 100, orderBy: volumeUSD, orderDirection: desc) {\n            id\n            symbol\n            name\n            derivedETH\n            derivedUSD\n          }\n        }\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "query": query\n        }\n        tokens = {}\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.post(self.endpoint, json=payload, headers=headers, timeout=15) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        print(f"[{self.name}] Error: Received status {resp.status}")\n                        return tokens\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    for token in data.get("data", {}).get("tokens", []):\n                        contract_address = token.get("id")\n                        name = token.get("name") or token.get("symbol") or contract_address\n                        # Prefer derivedUSD if available, else fallback to derivedETH (approximate with ETH price)\n                        price = None\n                        if token.get("derivedUSD") is not None:\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedUSD"])\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        elif token.get("derivedETH") is not None:\n                            # Fallback: Use ETH price from price=price,\n                                exchange=self.name\n                            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f"[{self.name}] Exception during fetch: {e}")\n        return tokens\n\n    async def _get_eth_price(self) -> float:\n        """\n        Fetches the current ETH price in USD from CoinGecko.\n        Used as a fallback if derivedUSD is not available.\n        """\n        url = "https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=ethereum&vs_currencies=usd"\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.get(url, timeout=10) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        return 0.0\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    return /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/real_time_token_monitoring_dashboard/dex_fetcher.py has been successfully created, with content:\nimport asyncio\nimport aiohttp\nfrom typing import Dict\n\nclass TokenData:\n    def __init__(self, name: str, contract_address: str, price: float, exchange: str):\n        self.name = name\n        self.contract_address = contract_address\n        self.price = price\n        self.exchange = exchange\n\nclass DEXFetcher:\n    def __init__(self, name: str, endpoint: str):\n        self.name = name\n        self.endpoint = endpoint\n\n    async def fetch_tokens(self) -> Dict[str, TokenData]:\n        """\n        Fetches token data from the DEX GraphQL endpoint.\n        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to TokenData.\n        """\n        # GraphQL queries for each DEX (Uniswap, PancakeSwap, Sushiswap)\n        # These queries fetch top tokens by liquidity and their price.\n        # For production, you may want to paginate or fetch more tokens.\n        query = """\n        {\n          tokens(first: 100, orderBy: volumeUSD, orderDirection: desc) {\n            id\n            symbol\n            name\n            derivedETH\n            derivedUSD\n          }\n        }\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "query": query\n        }\n        tokens = {}\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.post(self.endpoint, json=payload, headers=headers, timeout=15) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        print(f"[{self.name}] Error: Received status {resp.status}")\n                        return tokens\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    for token in data.get("data", {}).get("tokens", []):\n                        contract_address = token.get("id")\n                        name = token.get("name") or token.get("symbol") or contract_address\n                        # Prefer derivedUSD if available, else fallback to derivedETH (approximate with ETH price)\n                        price = None\n                        if token.get("derivedUSD") is not None:\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedUSD"])\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        elif token.get("derivedETH") is not None:\n                            # Fallback: Use ETH price from CoinGecko (could cache for efficiency)\n                            eth_price = await self._get_eth_price()\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedETH"]) * eth_price\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        if price is not None:\n                            tokens[contract_address] = TokenData(\n                                name=name,\n                                contract_address=contract_address,\n                                price=price,\n                                exchange=self.name\n                            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f"[{self.name}] Exception during fetch: {e}")\n        return tokens\n\n    async def _get_eth_price(self) -> float:\n        """\n        Fetches the current ETH price in USD from CoinGecko.\n        Used as a fallback if derivedUSD is not available.\n        """\n        url = "https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=ethereum&vs_currencies=usd"\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.get(url, timeout=10) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        return 0.0\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    return float(data.get("ethereum", {}).get("usd", 0.0))\n        except Exception:\n            return 0.0\n\n# Example DEX endpoints (mainnet)\nUNISWAP_V3_ENDPOINT = "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/uniswap/uniswap-v3"\nPANCAKESWAP_ENDPOINT = "https://bsc.streamingfast.io/subgraphs/name/pancakeswap/exchange-v2"\nSUSHISWAP_ENDPOINT = "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/sushiswap/exchange"\n\n# For testing purposes\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    async def main():\n        fetchers = [\n            DEXFetcher("Uniswap", UNISWAP_V3_ENDPOINT),\n            DEXFetcher("PancakeSwap", PANCAKESWAP_ENDPOINT),\n            DEXFetcher("Sushiswap", SUSHISWAP_ENDPOINT)\n        ]\n        results = await asyncio.gather(*(f.fetch_tokens() for f in fetchers))\n        for dex, tokens in zip(fetchers, results):\n            print(f"--- {dex.name} ---")\n            for t in list(tokens.values())[:5]:  # fetch more tokens.\n        query = """\n        {\n          tokens(first: 100, orderBy: volumeUSD, orderDirection: desc) {\n            id\n            symbol\n            name\n            derivedETH\n            derivedUSD\n          }\n        }\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "query": query\n        }\n        tokens = {}\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.post(self.endpoint, json=payload, headers=headers, timeout=15) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        print(f"[{self.name}] Error: Received status {resp.status}")\n                        return tokens\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    for token in data.get("data", {}).get("tokens", []):\n                        contract_address = token.get("id")\n                        name = token.get("name") or token.get("symbol") or contract_address\n                        # Prefer derivedUSD if available, else fallback to derivedETH (approximate with ETH price)\n                        price = None\n                        if token.get("derivedUSD") is not None:\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedUSD"])\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        elif token.get("derivedETH") is not None:\n                            # Fallback: Use ETH price from CoinGecko (could cache for efficiency)\n                            eth_price = await self._get_eth_price()\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedETH"]) * eth_price\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        if price is not None:\n  return tokens\n\n    async def _get_eth_price(self) -> float:\n        """\n        Fetches the current ETH price in USD from CoinGecko.\n        Used as a fallback if derivedUSD is not available.\n        """\n        url = "https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=ethereum&vs_currencies=usd"\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.get(url, timeout=10) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        return 0.0\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    return from typing import List, Dict, Optional
from dex_fetcher import TokenData

class ArbitrageOpportunity:
    def __init__(
        self,
        token_name: str,
        contract_address: str,
        lowest_price: float,
        highest_price: float,
        lowest_exchange: str,
        highest_exchange: str,
        price_diff_percent: float,
    ):
        self.token_name = token_name
        self.contract_address = contract_address
        self.lowest_price = lowest_price
        self.highest_price = highest_price
        self.lowest_exchange = lowest_exchange
        self.highest_exchange = highest_exchange
        self.price_diff_percent = price_diff_percent

class TokenAnalyzer:
    def __init__(self, dex_fetchers: List):
        """
        dex_fetchers: List of DEXFetcher instances
        """
        self.dex_fetchers = dex_fetchers

    def get_common_tokens(
        self, tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]]
    ) -> Dict[str, List[TokenData]]:
        """
        Finds tokens available on at least two exchanges.
        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to list of TokenData (from different exchanges).
        """
        token_map: Dict[str, List[TokenData]] = {}
        for dex_tokens in tokens_by_dex:
            for contract_address, token_data in dex_tokens.items():
                if contract_address not in token_map:
                    token_map[contract_address] = []
                token_map[contract_address].append(token_data)
        # Filter: only tokens present on at least two exchanges
        common_tokens = {
            k: v for k, v in token_map.items() if len(v) >= 2
        }
        return common_tokens

    def find_arbitrage_opportunities(
        self,
        tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]],
        threshold: float = 1.8,
    ) -> List[ArbitrageOpportunity]:
        """
        Finds arbitrage opportunities for tokens available on at least two exchanges.
        Returns a list of ArbitrageOpportunity objects where price difference > threshold (%).
        """
        opportunities: List[ArbitrageOpportunity] = []
        common_tokens = self.get_common_tokens(tokens_by_dex)
        for contract_address, token_datas in common_tokens.items():
            # Find lowest and highest price and corresponding exchanges
            lowest: Optional[TokenData] = None
            highest: Optional[TokenData] = None
            for td in token_datas:
                if lowest is None or td.price < lowest.price:
                    lowest = td
                if highest is None or td.price > highest.price:
                    highest = td
            if lowest is None or highest is None or lowest.price == 0:
                continue
            price_diff_percent = ((highest.price - lowest.price) / lowest.price) * 100
            if price_diff_percent > threshold:
                opportunity = ArbitrageOpportunity(
                    token_name=lowest.name,
                    contract_address=contract_address,
                    lowest_price=lowest.price,
                    highest_price=highest.price,
                    lowest_exchange=lowest.exchange,
                    highest_exchange=highest.exchange,
                    price_diff_percent=price_diff_percent,
                )
                opportunities.append(opportunity)
        return opportunities

Dict\n\nclass TokenData:\n    def __init__(self, name: str, contract_address: str, price: float, exchange: str):\n        self.name = name\n        self.contract_address = contract_address\n        self.price = price\n        self.exchange = exchange\n\nclass DEXFetcher:\n    def __init__(self, name: str, endpoint: str):\n        self.name = name\n        self.endpoint = endpoint\n\n    async def fetch_tokens(self) -> Dict[str, TokenData]:\n        """\n        Fetches token data from the DEX GraphQL endpoint.\n        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to TokenData.\n        """\n        # GraphQL queries for each DEX (Uniswap, PancakeSwap, Sushiswap)\n        # These queries fetch top tokens by liquidity and their price.\n        # For production, you may want to paginate or fetch more tokens.\n        query = """\n        {\n          tokens(first: 100, orderBy: volumeUSD, orderDirection: desc) {\n            id\n            symbol\n            name\n            derivedETH\n            derivedUSD\n          }\n        }\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "query": query\n        }\n        tokens = {}\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.post(self.endpoint, json=payload, headers=headers, timeout=15) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        print(f"[{self.name}] Error: Received status {resp.status}")\n                        return tokens\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    for token in data.get("data", {}).get("tokens", []):\n                        contract_address = token.get("id")\n                        name = token.get("name") or token.get("symbol") or contract_address\n                        # Prefer derivedUSD if available, else fallback to derivedETH (approximate with ETH price)\n                        price = None\n                        if token.get("derivedUSD") is not None:\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedUSD"])\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        elif token.get("derivedETH") is not None:\n                            # Fallback: Use ETH price from        tokens[contract_address] = TokenData(\n                                name=name,\n                                contract_address=contract_address,\n                                price=price,\n                                exchange=self.name\n                            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f"[{self.name}] Exception during fetch: {e}")\n        return tokens\n\n    async def _get_eth_price(self) -> float:\n        """\n        Fetches the current ETH price in USD from CoinGecko.\n        Used as a fallback if derivedUSD is not available.\n        """\n        url = "https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=ethereum&vs_currencies=usd"\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.get(url, timeout=10) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        return 0.0\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    return float(data.get("ethereum", {}).get("usd", 0.0))\n        except Exception:\n            return 0.0\n\n# Example DEX endpoints (mainnet)\nUNISWAP_V3_ENDPOINT = Filter: only tokens present on at least two exchanges\n        common_tokens = {\n            k: v for k, v in token_map.items() if len(v) >= 2\n        }\n        return common_tokens\n\n    def find_arbitrage_opportunities(\n        self,\n        tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]],\n        threshold: float = 1.8,\n    ) -> List[ArbitrageOpportunity]:\n        """\n        Finds arbitrage opportunities for tokens available on at least two exchanges.\n        Returns a list of ArbitrageOpportunity objects where price difference > threshold (%).\n        """\n        opportunities: List[ArbitrageOpportunity] = []\n        common_tokens = self.get_common_tokens(tokens_by_dex)\n        for contract_address, token_datas in common_tokens.items():\n            # Find lowest and highest price and corresponding exchanges\n            lowest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            highest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            for td in token_datas:\n                if lowest is None or td.price < lowest.price:\n                    lowest = td\n                if highest is None or td.price > highest.price:\n                    highest = td\n            if lowest is None or highest is None or lowest.price == 0:\n                continue\n            price_diff_percent = ((highest.price - lowest.price) / lowest.price) * 100\n            if price_diff_percent > threshold:\n                opportunity = ArbitrageOpportunity(\n                    token_name=lowest.name,\n                    contract_address=contract_address,\n                    lowest_price=lowest.price,\n                    highest_price=highest.price,\n                    lowest_exchange=lowest.exchange,\n                    highest_exchange=highest.exchange,\n                    price_diff_percent=price_diff_percent,\n                )\n                opportunities.append(opportunity)\n        return opportunities'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': '\n# Past Experience\n<experience>\n\n# Tool State\nThe current editor state is:\n(Current directory: /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace)\n(Open file: None)\n\n# Current Plan\n[GOAL] [Message] from Mike to Alex: Please implement the Python dashboard that fetches token data from Uniswap, PancakeSwap, and Sushiswap, analyzes tokens available on at least two exchanges, displays their names, contract addresses, lowest and highest price exchanges, and price differences greater than 1.8%, updating every second. def fetch_tokens(self) -> Dict[str, TokenData]:\n        """\n        Fetches token data from the DEX GraphQL endpoint.\n        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to TokenData.\n        """\n        # GraphQL queries for each DEX (Uniswap, PancakeSwap, Sushiswap)\n        # These queries fetch top tokens by liquidity and their price.\n        # For production, you may want to paginate or fetch more tokens.\n        query = """\n        {\n          tokens(first: 100, orderBy: volumeUSD, orderDirection: desc) {\n            id\n            symbol\n            name\n            derivedETH\n            derivedUSD\n          }\n        }\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "query": query\n        }\n        tokens = {}\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.post(self.endpoint, json=payload, headers=headers, timeout=15) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        print(f"[{self.name}] Error: Received status {resp.status}")\n                        return tokens\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    for token in data.get("data", {}).get("tokens", []):\n                        contract_address = token.get("id")\n                        name = token.get("name") or token.get("symbol") or contract_address\n                        # Prefer derivedUSD if available, else fallback to derivedETH (approximate with ETH price)\n                        price = None\n                        if token.get("derivedUSD") is not None:\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedUSD"])\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        elif token.get("derivedETH") is not None:\n                            # Fallback: Use ETH price from price=price,\n                                exchange=self.name\n                            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f"[{self.name}] Exception during fetch: {e}")\n        return tokens\n\n    async def _get_eth_price(self) -> float:\n        """\n        Fetches the current ETH price in USD from CoinGecko.\n        Used as a fallback if derivedUSD is not available.\n        """\n        url = "https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=ethereum&vs_currencies=usd"\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.get(url, timeout=10) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        return 0.0\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    return float(data.get("ethereum", {}).get("usd", 0.0))\n        except Exception:\n            return 0.0\n\n# Example DEX endpoints (mainnet)\nUNISWAP_V3_ENDPOINT = Filter: only tokens present on at least two exchanges\n        common_tokens = {\n            k: v for k, v in token_map.items() if len(v) >= 2\n        }\n        return common_tokens\n\n    def find_arbitrage_opportunities(\n        self,\n        tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]],\n        threshold: float = 1.8,\n    ) -> List[ArbitrageOpportunity]:\n        """\n        Finds arbitrage opportunities for tokens available on at least two exchanges.\n        Returns a list of ArbitrageOpportunity objects where price difference > threshold (%).\n        """\n        opportunities: List[ArbitrageOpportunity] = []\n        common_tokens = self.get_common_tokens(tokens_by_dex)\n        for contract_address, token_datas in common_tokens.items():\n            # Find lowest and highest price and corresponding exchanges\n            lowest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            highest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            for td in token_datas:\n                if lowest is None or td.price < lowest.price:\n                    lowest = td\n                if highest is None or td.price > highest.price:\n                    highest = td\n            if lowest is None or highest is None or lowest.price == 0:\n                continue\n            price_diff_percent = ((highest.price - lowest.price) / lowest.price) * 100\n            if price_diff_percent > threshold:\n                opportunity = ArbitrageOpportunity(\n                    token_name=lowest.name,\n                    contract_address=contract_address,\n                    lowest_price=lowest.price,\n                    highest_price=highest.price,\n                    lowest_exchange=lowest.exchange,\n                    highest_exchange=highest.exchange,\n                    price_diff_percent=price_diff_percent,\n                )\n                opportunities.append(opportunity)\n        return opportunities'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of token_analyzer.py is complete, as the TokenAnalyzer, TokenData, and ArbitrageOpportunity classes have been written according to the system design. highest prices so that I can act quickly.\\n021|\\n022|### 2.3 Competitive Analysis\\n023|| Product                | Pros                                              | Cons                                      |\\n024||------------------------|--------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------|\\n025|| DexTools               | Real-time data, multi-DEX Dict\n\nclass TokenData:\n    def __init__(self, name: str, contract_address: str, price: float, exchange: str):\n        self.name = name\n        self.contract_address = contract_address\n        self.price = price\n        self.exchange = exchange\n\nclass DEXFetcher:\n    def __init__(self, name: str, endpoint: str):\n        self.name = name\n        self.endpoint = endpoint\n\n    async def fetch_tokens(self) -> Dict[str, TokenData]:\n        """\n        Fetches token data from the DEX GraphQL endpoint.\n        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to TokenData.\n        """\n        # GraphQL queries for each DEX (Uniswap, PancakeSwap, Sushiswap)\n        # These queries fetch top tokens by liquidity and their price.\n        # For production, you may want to paginate or fetch more tokens.\n        query = """\n        {\n          tokens(first: 100, orderBy: volumeUSD, orderDirection: desc) {\n            id\n            symbol\n            name\n            derivedETH\n            derivedUSD\n          }\n        }\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "query": query\n        }\n        tokens = {}\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.post(self.endpoint, json=payload, headers=headers, timeout=15) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        print(f"[{self.name}] Error: Received status {resp.status}")\n                        return tokens\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    for token in data.get("data", {}).get("tokens", []):\n                        contract_address = token.get("id")\n                        name = token.get("name") or token.get("symbol") or contract_address\n                        # Prefer derivedUSD if available, else fallback to derivedETH (approximate with ETH price)\n                        price = None\n                        if token.get("derivedUSD") is not None:\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedUSD"])\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        elif token.get("derivedETH") is not None:\n                            # Fallback: Use ETH price from CoinGecko (could cache for efficiency)\n                            eth_price = await self._get_eth_price()\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedETH"]) * eth_price\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        if price is not None:\n                            tokens[contract_address] = TokenData(\n                                name=name,\n                                contract_address=contract_address,\n                                price=price,\n                                exchange=self.name\n                            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f"[{self.name}] Exception during fetch: {e}")\n        return tokens\n\n    async def _get_eth_price(self) -> float:\n        """\n        Fetches the current ETH price in USD from CoinGecko.\n        Used as a fallback if derivedUSD is not available.\n        """\n        url = "https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=ethereum&vs_currencies=usd"\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.get(url, timeout=10) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        return 0.0\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    return float(data.get("ethereum", {}).get("usd", 0.0))\n        except Exception:\n            return 0.0\n\n# Example DEX endpoints (mainnet)\nUNISWAP_V3_ENDPOINT = "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/uniswap/uniswap-v3"\nPANCAKESWAP_ENDPOINT = "https://bsc.streamingfast.io/subgraphs/name/pancakeswap/exchange-v2"\nSUSHISWAP_ENDPOINT = "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/sushiswap/exchange"\n\n# For testing purposes\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    async def main():\n        fetchers = [\n            DEXFetcher("Uniswap", UNISWAP_V3_ENDPOINT),\n            DEXFetcher("PancakeSwap", PANCAKESWAP_ENDPOINT),\n            DEXFetcher("Sushiswap", SUSHISWAP_ENDPOINT)\n        ]\n        results = await asyncio.gather(*(f.fetch_tokens() for f in fetchers))\n        for dex, tokens in zip(fetchers, results):\n            print(f"--- {dex.name} ---")\n            for t in list(tokens.values())[:5]:  # TokenAnalyzer analyzes tokens from multiple DEXes, finds tokens available on at least two exchanges, and identifies arbitrage opportunities with price differences greater than a threshold."\n        }\n    }\n]\n```'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': 'Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/real_time_token_monitoring_dashboard/token_analyzer.py has been successfully created, with content:\nfrom typing import List, Dict, Optional\nfrom dex_fetcher import TokenData\n\nclass ArbitrageOpportunity:\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        token_name: str,\n        contract_address: str,\n        lowest_price: float,\n        highest_price: float,\n        lowest_exchange: str,\n        highest_exchange: str,\n        price_diff_percent: float,\n    ):\n        self.token_name = token_name\n        self.contract_address = contract_address\n        self.lowest_price = lowest_price\n        self.highest_price = highest_price\n        self.lowest_exchange = lowest_exchange\n        self.highest_exchange = highest_exchange\n        self.price_diff_percent = price_diff_percent\n\nclass TokenAnalyzer:\n    def __init__(self, dex_fetchers: List):\n        """\n        dex_fetchers: List of DEXFetcher instances\n        """\n        self.dex_fetchers = dex_fetchers\n\n    def get_common_tokens(\n        self, tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]]\n    ) -> Dict[str, List[TokenData]]:\n        """\n        Finds tokens available on at least two exchanges.\n        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to list of TokenData (from different exchanges).\n        """\n        token_map: Dict[str, List[TokenData]] = {}\n        for dex_tokens in tokens_by_dex:\n            for contract_address, token_data in dex_tokens.items():\n                if contract_address not in token_map:\n                    token_map[contract_address] = []\n                token_map[contract_address].append(token_data)\n        # Filter: only tokens present on at least two exchanges\n        common_tokens = {\n            k: v for k, v in token_map.items() if len(v) >= 2\n        }\n        return common_tokens\n\n    def find_arbitrage_opportunities(\n        self,\n        tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]],\n        threshold: float = 1.8,\n    ) -> List[ArbitrageOpportunity]:\n        """\n        Finds arbitrage opportunities for tokens available on at least two exchanges.\n        Returns a list of ArbitrageOpportunity objects where price difference > threshold (%).\n        """\n        opportunities: List[ArbitrageOpportunity] = []\n        common_tokens = self.get_common_tokens(tokens_by_dex)\n        for contract_address, token_datas in common_tokens.items():\n            # Find lowest and highest price and corresponding exchanges\n            lowest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            highest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            for td in token_datas:\n                if lowest is None or td.price < lowest.price:\n                    lowest = td\n                if highest is None or td.price > highest.price:\n                    highest = td\n            if lowest is None or highest is None or lowest.price == 0:\n                continue\n            price_diff_percent = ((highest.price - lowest.price) / lowest.price) * 100\n            if price_diff_percent > threshold:\n                opportunity = ArbitrageOpportunity(\n                    token_name=lowest.name,\n                    contract_address=contract_address,\n                    lowest_price=lowest.price,\n                    highest_price=highest.price,\n                    lowest_exchange=lowest.exchange,\n                    highest_exchange=highest.exchange,\n                    price_diff_percent=price_diff_percent,\n                )\n                opportunities.append(opportunity)\n        return opportunities'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of token_analyzer.py is complete, as the TokenAnalyzer, TokenData, and ArbitrageOpportunity classes have been written according to the system design. 2025-9-20 13:44:15.983 | INFO     | metagpt.roles.di.role_zero:_act:294 - Commands outputs: 
Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/real_time_token_monitoring_dashboard/dashboard.py has been successfully created, with content:
import streamlit as st
import pandas as pd
from typing import List
from token_analyzer import ArbitrageOpportunity, TokenAnalyzer
import time

class Dashboard:
    def __init__(self, analyzer: TokenAnalyzer):
        self.analyzer = analyzer

    def render_table(self, opportunities: List[ArbitrageOpportunity]):
        """
        Renders the arbitrage opportunities in a Streamlit table.
        """
        if not opportunities:
            st.info("No arbitrage opportunities found with price difference > 1.8%.")
            return

        # Prepare data for DataFrame
        data = []
        for opp in opportunities:
            data.append({
                "Token Name": opp.token_name,
                "Contract Address": opp.contract_address,
                "Lowest Price Exchange": opp.lowest_exchange,
                "Lowest Price (USD)": f"${opp.lowest_price:.6f}",
                "Highest Price Exchange": opp.highest_exchange,
                "Highest Price (USD)": f"${opp.highest_price:.6f}",
                "Price Difference (%)": f"{opp.price_diff_percent:.2f}%",
            })
        df = pd.DataFrame(data)
        st.dataframe(df, use_container_width=True)

    def run(self, fetch_and_analyze_callback):
        """
        Main dashboard loop. Calls fetch_and_analyze_callback to get arbitrage opportunities.
        Updates every second.
        """
        st.set_page_config(page_title="Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard", layout="wide")
        st.title("Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard")
        st.caption("Monitoring Uniswap, PancakeSwap, and Sushiswap for arbitrage opportunities (refreshes every second)")

        refresh_interval = 1  # seconds

        # Streamlit's session state to persist data between reruns
        if "last_update" not in st.session_state:
            st.session_state["last_update"] = 0

        # Main loop
        while True:
            start_time = time.time()
            with st.spinner("Fetching latest token data and analyzing opportunities..."):
                opportunities = fetch_and_analyze_callback()
            st.subheader("Arbitrage Opportunities (>1.8% price difference)")
            self.render_table(opportunities)
            st.write(f"Last updated: {time.strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}")
            # Wait for the next refresh
            elapsed = time.time() - start_time
            if elapsed < refresh_interval:
                time.sleep(refresh_interval - elapsed)
            # Rerun Streamlit script
            st.experimental_rerun()

# Example usage (for main.py):
# from token_analyzer import TokenAnalyzer
# analyzer = TokenAnalyzer([...])
# dashboard = Dashboard(analyzer)
# dashboard.run(fetch_and_analyze_callback)

def fetch_tokens(self) -> Dict[str, TokenData]:\n        """\n        Fetches token data from the DEX GraphQL endpoint.\n        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to TokenData.\n        """\n        # GraphQL queries for each DEX (Uniswap, PancakeSwap, Sushiswap)\n        # These queries fetch top tokens by liquidity and their price.\n        # For production, you may want to paginate or fetch more tokens.\n        query = """\n        {\n          tokens(first: 100, orderBy: volumeUSD, orderDirection: desc) {\n            id\n            symbol\n            name\n            derivedETH\n            derivedUSD\n          }\n        }\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "query": query\n        }\n        tokens = {}\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.post(self.endpoint, json=payload, headers=headers, timeout=15) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        print(f"[{self.name}] Error: Received status {resp.status}")\n                        return tokens\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    for token in data.get("data", {}).get("tokens", []):\n                        contract_address = token.get("id")\n                        name = token.get("name") or token.get("symbol") or contract_address\n                        # Prefer derivedUSD if available, else fallback to derivedETH (approximate with ETH price)\n                        price = None\n                        if token.get("derivedUSD") is not None:\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedUSD"])\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        elif token.get("derivedETH") is not None:\n                            # Fallback: Use ETH price from        tokens[contract_address] = TokenData(\n                                name=name,\n                                contract_address=contract_address,\n                                price=price,\n                                exchange=self.name\n                            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f"[{self.name}] Exception during fetch: {e}")\n        return tokens\n\n    async def _get_eth_price(self) -> float:\n        """\n        Fetches the current ETH price in USD from CoinGecko.\n        Used as a fallback if derivedUSD is not available.\n        """\n        url = "https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=ethereum&vs_currencies=usd"\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.get(url, timeout=10) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        return 0.0\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    return Filter: only tokens present on at least two exchanges\n        common_tokens = {\n            k: v for k, v in token_map.items() if len(v) >= 2\n        }\n        return common_tokens\n\n    def find_arbitrage_opportunities(\n        self,\n        tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]],\n        threshold: float = 1.8,\n    ) -> List[ArbitrageOpportunity]:\n        """\n        Finds arbitrage opportunities for tokens available on at least two exchanges.\n        Returns a list of ArbitrageOpportunity objects where price difference > threshold (%).\n        """\n        opportunities: List[ArbitrageOpportunity] = []\n        common_tokens = self.get_common_tokens(tokens_by_dex)\n        for contract_address, token_datas in common_tokens.items():\n            # Find lowest and highest price and corresponding exchanges\n            lowest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            highest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            for td in token_datas:\n                if lowest is None or td.price < lowest.price:\n                    lowest = td\n                if highest is None or td.price > highest.price:\n                    highest = td\n            if lowest is None or highest is None or lowest.price == 0:\n                continue\n            price_diff_percent = ((highest.price - lowest.price) / lowest.price) * 100\n            if price_diff_percent > threshold:\n                opportunity = ArbitrageOpportunity(\n                    token_name=lowest.name,\n                    contract_address=contract_address,\n                    lowest_price=lowest.price,\n                    highest_price=highest.price,\n                    lowest_exchange=lowest.exchange,\n                    highest_exchange=highest.exchange,\n                    price_diff_percent=price_diff_percent,\n                )\n                opportunities.append(opportunity)\n        return opportunities'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of token_analyzer.py is complete, as the TokenAnalyzer, TokenData, and ArbitrageOpportunity classes have been written according to the system design. Calls fetch_and_analyze_callback to get arbitrage opportunities.\n        Updates every second.\n        """\n        st.set_page_config(page_title="Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard", layout="wide")\n        st.title("Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard")\n        st.caption("Monitoring Uniswap, PancakeSwap, and Sushiswap for arbitrage opportunities (refreshes every second)")\n\n        refresh_interval = 1  # seconds\n\n        # Streamlit\'s session state to persist data between reruns\n        if "last_update" not in st.session_state:\n            st.session_state["last_update"] = 0\n\n        # Main loop\n        while True:\n            start_time = time.time()\n            with st.spinner("Fetching latest token data and analyzing opportunities..."):\n                opportunities = fetch_and_analyze_callback()\n            st.subheader("Arbitrage Opportunities (>1.8% price difference)")\n            self.render_table(opportunities)\n            st.write(f"Last updated: {time.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\')}")\n            # Wait for the next refresh\n            elapsed = time.time() - start_time\n            if elapsed < refresh_interval:\n                time.sleep(refresh_interval - elapsed)\n            # Rerun Streamlit script\n            st.experimental_rerun()\n\n# Example usage (for main.py):\n# from token_analyzer import TokenAnalyzer\n# analyzer = TokenAnalyzer([...])\n# dashboard = Dashboard(analyzer)\n# dashboard.run(fetch_and_analyze_callback)'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': '\n# Past Experience\n<experience>\n\n# Tool State\nThe current editor state is:\n(Current directory: /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace)\n(Open file: None)\n\n# Current Plan\n[GOAL] [Message] from Mike to Alex: Please implement the Python dashboard that fetches token data from Uniswap, PancakeSwap, and Sushiswap, analyzes tokens available on at least two exchanges, displays their names, contract addresses, lowest and highest price exchanges, and price differences greater than 1.8%, updating every second. def fetch_tokens(self) -> Dict[str, TokenData]:\n        """\n        Fetches token data from the DEX GraphQL endpoint.\n        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to TokenData.\n        """\n        # GraphQL queries for each DEX (Uniswap, PancakeSwap, Sushiswap)\n        # These queries fetch top tokens by liquidity and their price.\n        # For production, you may want to paginate or derivedETH\n            derivedUSD\n          }\n        }\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "query": query\n        }\n        tokens = {}\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.post(self.endpoint, json=payload, headers=headers, timeout=15) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        print(f"[{self.name}] Error: Received status {resp.status}")\n                        return tokens\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    for token in data.get("data", {}).get("tokens", []):\n                        contract_address = token.get("id")\n                        name = token.get("name") or token.get("symbol") or contract_address\n                        # Prefer derivedUSD if available, else fallback to derivedETH (approximate with ETH price)\n                        price = None\n                        if token.get("derivedUSD") is not None:\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedUSD"])\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        elif token.get("derivedETH") is not None:\n                            # Fallback: Use ETH price from price=price,\n                                exchange=self.name\n                            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f"[{self.name}] Exception during fetch: {e}")\n        return tokens\n\n    async def _get_eth_price(self) -> float:\n        """\n        Fetches the current ETH price in USD from CoinGecko.\n        Used as a fallback if derivedUSD is not available.\n        """\n        url = "https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=ethereum&vs_currencies=usd"\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.get(url, timeout=10) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        return 0.0\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    return Filter: only tokens present on at least two exchanges\n        common_tokens = {\n            k: v for k, v in token_map.items() if len(v) >= 2\n        }\n        return common_tokens\n\n    def find_arbitrage_opportunities(\n        self,\n        tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]],\n        threshold: float = 1.8,\n    ) -> List[ArbitrageOpportunity]:\n        """\n        Finds arbitrage opportunities for tokens available on at least two exchanges.\n        Returns a list of ArbitrageOpportunity objects where price difference > threshold (%).\n        """\n        opportunities: List[ArbitrageOpportunity] = []\n        common_tokens = self.get_common_tokens(tokens_by_dex)\n        for contract_address, token_datas in common_tokens.items():\n            # Find lowest and highest price and corresponding exchanges\n            lowest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            highest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            for td in token_datas:\n                if lowest is None or td.price < lowest.price:\n                    lowest = td\n                if highest is None or td.price > highest.price:\n                    highest = td\n            if lowest is None or highest is None or lowest.price == 0:\n                continue\n            price_diff_percent = ((highest.price - lowest.price) / lowest.price) * 100\n            if price_diff_percent > threshold:\n                opportunity = ArbitrageOpportunity(\n                    token_name=lowest.name,\n                    contract_address=contract_address,\n                    lowest_price=lowest.price,\n                    highest_price=highest.price,\n                    lowest_exchange=lowest.exchange,\n                    highest_exchange=highest.exchange,\n                    price_diff_percent=price_diff_percent,\n                )\n                opportunities.append(opportunity)\n        return opportunities'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of token_analyzer.py is complete, as the TokenAnalyzer, TokenData, and ArbitrageOpportunity classes have been written according to the system design. Calls fetch_and_analyze_callback to get arbitrage opportunities.\n        Updates every second.\n        """\n        st.set_page_config(page_title="Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard", layout="wide")\n        st.title("Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard")\n        st.caption("Monitoring Uniswap, PancakeSwap, and Sushiswap for arbitrage opportunities (refreshes every second)")\n\n        refresh_interval = 1  # seconds\n\n        # Streamlit\'s session state to persist data between reruns\n        if "last_update" not in st.session_state:\n            st.session_state["last_update"] = 0\n\n        # Main loop\n        while True:\n            start_time = time.time()\n            with st.spinner("Fetching latest token data and analyzing opportunities..."):\n                opportunities = fetch_and_analyze_callback()\n            st.subheader("Arbitrage Opportunities (>1.8% price difference)")\n            self.render_table(opportunities)\n            st.write(f"Last updated: {time.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\')}")\n            # Wait for the next refresh\n            elapsed = time.time() - start_time\n            if elapsed < refresh_interval:\n                time.sleep(refresh_interval - elapsed)\n            # Rerun Streamlit script\n            st.experimental_rerun()\n\n# Example usage (for main.py):\n# from token_analyzer import TokenAnalyzer\n# analyzer = TokenAnalyzer([...])\n# dashboard = Dashboard(analyzer)\n# dashboard.run(fetch_and_analyze_callback)'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of dashboard.py is complete, as the Dashboard class using Streamlit has been written. Dict\n\nclass TokenData:\n    def __init__(self, name: str, contract_address: str, price: float, exchange: str):\n        self.name = name\n        self.contract_address = contract_address\n        self.price = price\n        self.exchange = exchange\n\nclass DEXFetcher:\n    def __init__(self, name: str, endpoint: str):\n        self.name = name\n        self.endpoint = endpoint\n\n    async def fetch_tokens(self) -> Dict[str, TokenData]:\n        """\n        Fetches token data from the DEX GraphQL endpoint.\n        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to TokenData.\n        """\n        # GraphQL queries for each DEX (Uniswap, PancakeSwap, Sushiswap)\n        # These queries fetch top tokens by liquidity and their price.\n        # For production, you may want to paginate or fetch more tokens.\n        query = """\n        {\n          tokens(first: 100, orderBy: volumeUSD, orderDirection: desc) {\n            id\n            symbol\n            name\n            derivedETH\n            derivedUSD\n          }\n        }\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "query": query\n        }\n        tokens = {}\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.post(self.endpoint, json=payload, headers=headers, timeout=15) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        print(f"[{self.name}] Error: Received status {resp.status}")\n                        return tokens\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    for token in data.get("data", {}).get("tokens", []):\n                        contract_address = token.get("id")\n                        name = token.get("name") or token.get("symbol") or contract_address\n                        # Prefer derivedUSD if available, else fallback to derivedETH (approximate with ETH price)\n                        price = None\n                        if token.get("derivedUSD") is not None:\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedUSD"])\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        elif token.get("derivedETH") is not None:\n                            # Fallback: Use ETH price from CoinGecko (could cache for efficiency)\n                            eth_price = await self._get_eth_price()\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedETH"]) * eth_price\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        if price is not None:\n                            tokens[contract_address] = TokenData(\n                                name=name,\n                                contract_address=contract_address,\n                                price=price,\n                                exchange=self.name\n                            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f"[{self.name}] Exception during fetch: {e}")\n        return tokens\n\n    async def _get_eth_price(self) -> float:\n        """\n        Fetches the current ETH price in USD from CoinGecko.\n        Used as a fallback if derivedUSD is not available.\n        """\n        url = "https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=ethereum&vs_currencies=usd"\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.get(url, timeout=10) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        return 0.0\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    return float(data.get("ethereum", {}).get("usd", 0.0))\n        except Exception:\n            return 0.0\n\n# Example DEX endpoints (mainnet)\nUNISWAP_V3_ENDPOINT = "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/uniswap/uniswap-v3"\nPANCAKESWAP_ENDPOINT = "https://bsc.streamingfast.io/subgraphs/name/pancakeswap/exchange-v2"\nSUSHISWAP_ENDPOINT = "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/sushiswap/exchange"\n\n# For testing purposes\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    async def main():\n        fetchers = [\n            DEXFetcher("Uniswap", UNISWAP_V3_ENDPOINT),\n            DEXFetcher("PancakeSwap", PANCAKESWAP_ENDPOINT),\n            DEXFetcher("Sushiswap", SUSHISWAP_ENDPOINT)\n        ]\n        results = await asyncio.gather(*(f.fetch_tokens() for f in fetchers))\n        for dex, tokens in zip(fetchers, results):\n            print(f"--- {dex.name} ---")\n            for t in list(tokens.values())[:5]:  # Print top 5 tokens for brevity\n                print(f"{t.name} ({t.contract_address}): ${t.price:.4f}")\n\n    asyncio.run(main())'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of TokenAnalyzer analyzes tokens from multiple DEXes, finds tokens available on at least two exchanges, and identifies arbitrage opportunities with price differences greater than a threshold."\n        }\n    }\n]\n```'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': 'Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/real_time_token_monitoring_dashboard/token_analyzer.py has been successfully created, with content:\nfrom typing import List, Dict, Optional\nfrom dex_fetcher import TokenData\n\nclass ArbitrageOpportunity:\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        token_name: str,\n        contract_address: str,\n        lowest_price: float,\n        highest_price: float,\n        lowest_exchange: str,\n        highest_exchange: str,\n        price_diff_percent: float,\n    ):\n        self.token_name = token_name\n        self.contract_address = contract_address\n        self.lowest_price = lowest_price\n        self.highest_price = highest_price\n        self.lowest_exchange = lowest_exchange\n        self.highest_exchange = highest_exchange\n        self.price_diff_percent = price_diff_percent\n\nclass TokenAnalyzer:\n    def __init__(self, dex_fetchers: List):\n        """\n        dex_fetchers: List of DEXFetcher instances\n        """\n        self.dex_fetchers = dex_fetchers\n\n    def get_common_tokens(\n        self, tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]]\n    ) -> Dict[str, List[TokenData]]:\n        """\n        Finds tokens available on at least two exchanges.\n        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to list of TokenData (from different exchanges).\n        """\n        token_map: Dict[str, List[TokenData]] = {}\n        for dex_tokens in tokens_by_dex:\n            for contract_address, token_data in dex_tokens.items():\n                if contract_address not in token_map:\n                    token_map[contract_address] = []\n                token_map[contract_address].append(token_data)\n        # Filter: only tokens present on at least two exchanges\n        common_tokens = {\n            k: v for k, v in token_map.items() if len(v) >= 2\n        }\n        return common_tokens\n\n    def find_arbitrage_opportunities(\n        self,\n        tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]],\n        threshold: float = 1.8,\n    ) -> List[ArbitrageOpportunity]:\n        """\n        Finds arbitrage opportunities for tokens available on at least two exchanges.\n        Returns a list of ArbitrageOpportunity objects where price difference > threshold (%).\n        """\n        opportunities: List[ArbitrageOpportunity] = []\n        common_tokens = self.get_common_tokens(tokens_by_dex)\n        for contract_address, token_datas in common_tokens.items():\n            # Find lowest and highest price and corresponding exchanges\n            lowest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            highest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            for td in token_datas:\n                if lowest is None or td.price < lowest.price:\n                    lowest = td\n                if highest is None or td.price > highest.price:\n                    highest = td\n            if lowest is None or highest is None or lowest.price == 0:\n                continue\n            price_diff_percent = ((highest.price - lowest.price) / lowest.price) * 100\n            if price_diff_percent > threshold:\n                opportunity = ArbitrageOpportunity(\n                    token_name=lowest.name,\n                    contract_address=contract_address,\n                    lowest_price=lowest.price,\n                    highest_price=highest.price,\n                    lowest_exchange=lowest.exchange,\n                    highest_exchange=highest.exchange,\n                    price_diff_percent=price_diff_percent,\n                )\n                opportunities.append(opportunity)\n        return opportunities'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of token_analyzer.py is complete, as the TokenAnalyzer, TokenData, and ArbitrageOpportunity classes have been written according to the system design. Calls fetch_and_analyze_callback to get arbitrage opportunities.\n        Updates every second.\n        """\n        st.set_page_config(page_title="Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard", layout="wide")\n        st.title("Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard")\n        st.caption("Monitoring Uniswap, PancakeSwap, and Sushiswap for arbitrage opportunities (refreshes every second)")\n\n        refresh_interval = 1  # seconds\n\n        # Streamlit\'s session state to persist data between reruns\n        if "last_update" not in st.session_state:\n            st.session_state["last_update"] = 0\n\n        # Main loop\n        while True:\n            start_time = time.time()\n            with st.spinner("Fetching latest token data and analyzing opportunities..."):\n                opportunities = fetch_and_analyze_callback()\n            st.subheader("Arbitrage Opportunities (>1.8% price difference)")\n            self.render_table(opportunities)\n            st.write(f"Last updated: {time.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\')}")\n            # Wait for the next refresh\n            elapsed = time.time() - start_time\n            if elapsed < refresh_interval:\n                time.sleep(refresh_interval - elapsed)\n            # Rerun Streamlit script\n            st.experimental_rerun()\n\n# Example usage (for main.py):\n# from token_analyzer import TokenAnalyzer\n# analyzer = TokenAnalyzer([...])\n# dashboard = Dashboard(analyzer)\n# dashboard.run(fetch_and_analyze_callback)'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of dashboard.py is complete, as the Dashboard class using Streamlit has been written. 2025-9-20 13:44:27.352 | INFO     | metagpt.roles.di.role_zero:_act:294 - Commands outputs: 
Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/real_time_token_monitoring_dashboard/main.py has been successfully created, with content:
import asyncio
from typing import List, Dict
from dex_fetcher import DEXFetcher, TokenData, UNISWAP_V3_ENDPOINT, PANCAKESWAP_ENDPOINT, SUSHISWAP_ENDPOINT
from token_analyzer import TokenAnalyzer, ArbitrageOpportunity
from dashboard import Dashboard
import streamlit as st

# Configuration
DEX_CONFIGS = [
    {"name": "Uniswap", "endpoint": UNISWAP_V3_ENDPOINT},
    {"name": "PancakeSwap", "endpoint": PANCAKESWAP_ENDPOINT},
    {"name": "Sushiswap", "endpoint": SUSHISWAP_ENDPOINT},
]
ARBITRAGE_THRESHOLD = 1.8  # percent

def create_dex_fetchers() -> List[DEXFetcher]:
    return [DEXFetcher(cfg["name"], cfg["endpoint"]) for cfg in DEX_CONFIGS]

async def fetch_all_tokens(dex_fetchers: List[DEXFetcher]) -> List[Dict[str, TokenData]]:
    return await asyncio.gather(*(dex.fetch_tokens() for dex in dex_fetchers))

def fetch_and_analyze_callback_factory(dex_fetchers, analyzer):
    """
    Returns a callback function for the dashboard to fetch and analyze arbitrage opportunities.
    This function will be called by the dashboard every second.
    """
    def fetch_and_analyze_callback():
        # Run async fetching in a synchronous context for Streamlit
        loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()
        asyncio.set_event_loop(loop)
        tokens_by_dex = loop.run_until_complete(fetch_all_tokens(dex_fetchers))
        opportunities = analyzer.find_arbitrage_opportunities(tokens_by_dex, threshold=ARBITRAGE_THRESHOLD)
        return opportunities
    return fetch_and_analyze_callback

def main():
    dex_fetchers = create_dex_fetchers()
    analyzer = TokenAnalyzer(dex_fetchers)
    dashboard = Dashboard(analyzer)
    fetch_and_analyze_callback = fetch_and_analyze_callback_factory(dex_fetchers, analyzer)
    dashboard.run(fetch_and_analyze_callback)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

/home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/real_time_token_monitoring_dashboard/dex_fetcher.py has been successfully created, with content:\nimport asyncio\nimport aiohttp\nfrom typing import Dict\n\nclass TokenData:\n    def __init__(self, name: str, contract_address: str, price: float, exchange: str):\n        self.name = name\n        self.contract_address = contract_address\n        self.price = price\n        self.exchange = exchange\n\nclass DEXFetcher:\n    def __init__(self, name: str, endpoint: str):\n        self.name = name\n        self.endpoint = endpoint\n\n    async def fetch_tokens(self) -> Dict[str, TokenData]:\n        """\n        Fetches token data from the DEX GraphQL endpoint.\n        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to TokenData.\n        """\n        # GraphQL queries for each DEX (Uniswap, PancakeSwap, Sushiswap)\n        # These queries fetch top tokens by liquidity and their price.\n        # For production, you may want to paginate or fetch more tokens.\n        query = """\n        {\n          tokens(first: 100, orderBy: volumeUSD, orderDirection: desc) {\n            id\n            symbol\n            name\n            derivedETH\n            derivedUSD\n          }\n        }\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "query": query\n        }\n        tokens = {}\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.post(self.endpoint, json=payload, headers=headers, timeout=15) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        print(f"[{self.name}] Error: Received status {resp.status}")\n                        return tokens\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    for token in data.get("data", {}).get("tokens", []):\n                        contract_address = token.get("id")\n                        name = token.get("name") or token.get("symbol") or contract_address\n                        # Prefer derivedUSD if available, else fallback to derivedETH (approximate with ETH price)\n                        price = None\n                        if token.get("derivedUSD") is not None:\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedUSD"])\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        elif token.get("derivedETH") is not None:\n                            # Fallback: Use ETH price from CoinGecko (could cache for efficiency)\n                            eth_price = await self._get_eth_price()\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedETH"]) * eth_price\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        if price is not None:\n                            tokens[contract_address] = TokenData(\n                                name=name,\n                                contract_address=contract_address,\n                                price=price,\n                                exchange=self.name\n                            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f"[{self.name}] Exception during fetch: {e}")\n        return tokens\n\n    async def _get_eth_price(self) -> float:\n        """\n        Fetches the current ETH price in USD from CoinGecko.\n        Used as a fallback if derivedUSD is not available.\n        """\n        url = "https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=ethereum&vs_currencies=usd"\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.get(url, timeout=10) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        return 0.0\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    return float(data.get("ethereum", {}).get("usd", 0.0))\n        except Exception:\n            return 0.0\n\n# Example DEX endpoints (mainnet)\nUNISWAP_V3_ENDPOINT = "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/uniswap/uniswap-v3"\nPANCAKESWAP_ENDPOINT = "https://bsc.streamingfast.io/subgraphs/name/pancakeswap/exchange-v2"\nSUSHISWAP_ENDPOINT = "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/sushiswap/exchange"\n\n# For testing purposes\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    async def main():\n        fetchers = [\n            DEXFetcher("Uniswap", UNISWAP_V3_ENDPOINT),\n            DEXFetcher("PancakeSwap", PANCAKESWAP_ENDPOINT),\n            DEXFetcher("Sushiswap", SUSHISWAP_ENDPOINT)\n        ]\n        results = await asyncio.gather(*(f.fetch_tokens() for f in fetchers))\n        for dex, tokens in zip(fetchers, results):\n            print(f"--- {dex.name} ---")\n            for t in list(tokens.values())[:5]:  # Print top 5 tokens for brevity\n                print(f"{t.name} ({t.contract_address}): ${t.price:.4f}")\n\n    asyncio.run(main())'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of
