TokenAnalyzer analyzes tokens from multiple DEXes, finds tokens available on at least two exchanges, and identifies arbitrage opportunities with price differences greater than a threshold."\n        }\n    }\n]\n```'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': 'Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/real_time_token_monitoring_dashboard/token_analyzer.py has been successfully created, with content:\nfrom typing import List, Dict, Optional\nfrom dex_fetcher import TokenData\n\nclass ArbitrageOpportunity:\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        token_name: str,\n        contract_address: str,\n        lowest_price: float,\n        highest_price: float,\n        lowest_exchange: str,\n        highest_exchange: str,\n        price_diff_percent: float,\n    ):\n        self.token_name = token_name\n        self.contract_address = contract_address\n        self.lowest_price = lowest_price\n        self.highest_price = highest_price\n        self.lowest_exchange = lowest_exchange\n        self.highest_exchange = highest_exchange\n        self.price_diff_percent = price_diff_percent\n\nclass TokenAnalyzer:\n    def __init__(self, dex_fetchers: List):\n        """\n        dex_fetchers: List of DEXFetcher instances\n        """\n        self.dex_fetchers = dex_fetchers\n\n    def get_common_tokens(\n        self, tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]]\n    ) -> Dict[str, List[TokenData]]:\n        """\n        Finds tokens available on at least two exchanges.\n        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to list of TokenData (from different exchanges).\n        """\n        token_map: Dict[str, List[TokenData]] = {}\n        for dex_tokens in tokens_by_dex:\n            for contract_address, token_data in dex_tokens.items():\n                if contract_address not in token_map:\n                    token_map[contract_address] = []\n                token_map[contract_address].append(token_data)\n        # Filter: only tokens present on at least two exchanges\n        common_tokens = {\n            k: v for k, v in token_map.items() if len(v) >= 2\n        }\n        return common_tokens\n\n    def find_arbitrage_opportunities(\n        self,\n        tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]],\n        threshold: float = 1.8,\n    ) -> List[ArbitrageOpportunity]:\n        """\n        Finds arbitrage opportunities for tokens available on at least two exchanges.\n        Returns a list of ArbitrageOpportunity objects where price difference > threshold (%).\n        """\n        opportunities: List[ArbitrageOpportunity] = []\n        common_tokens = self.get_common_tokens(tokens_by_dex)\n        for contract_address, token_datas in common_tokens.items():\n            # Find lowest and highest price and corresponding exchanges\n            lowest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            highest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            for td in token_datas:\n                if lowest is None or td.price < lowest.price:\n                    lowest = td\n                if highest is None or td.price > highest.price:\n                    highest = td\n            if lowest is None or highest is None or lowest.price == 0:\n                continue\n            price_diff_percent = ((highest.price - lowest.price) / lowest.price) * 100\n            if price_diff_percent > threshold:\n                opportunity = ArbitrageOpportunity(\n                    token_name=lowest.name,\n                    contract_address=contract_address,\n                    lowest_price=lowest.price,\n                    highest_price=highest.price,\n                    lowest_exchange=lowest.exchange,\n                    highest_exchange=highest.exchange,\n                    price_diff_percent=price_diff_percent,\n                )\n                opportunities.append(opportunity)\n        return opportunities'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of token_analyzer.py is complete, as the TokenAnalyzer, TokenData, and ArbitrageOpportunity classes have been written according to the system design. Calls fetch_and_analyze_callback to get arbitrage opportunities.\n        Updates every second.\n        """\n        st.set_page_config(page_title="Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard", layout="wide")\n        st.title("Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard")\n        st.caption("Monitoring Uniswap, PancakeSwap, and Sushiswap for arbitrage opportunities (refreshes every second)")\n\n        refresh_interval = 1  # seconds\n\n        # Streamlit\'s session state to persist data between reruns\n        if "last_update" not in st.session_state:\n            st.session_state["last_update"] = 0\n\n        # Main loop\n        while True:\n            start_time = time.time()\n            with st.spinner("Fetching latest token data and analyzing opportunities..."):\n                opportunities = fetch_and_analyze_callback()\n            st.subheader("Arbitrage Opportunities (>1.8% price difference)")\n            self.render_table(opportunities)\n            st.write(f"Last updated: {time.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\')}")\n            # Wait for the next refresh\n            elapsed = time.time() - start_time\n            if elapsed < refresh_interval:\n                time.sleep(refresh_interval - elapsed)\n            # Rerun Streamlit script\n            st.experimental_rerun()\n\n# Example usage (for main.py):\n# from token_analyzer import TokenAnalyzer\n# analyzer = TokenAnalyzer([...])\n# dashboard = Dashboard(analyzer)\n# dashboard.run(fetch_and_analyze_callback)'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of dashboard.py is complete, as the Dashboard class using Streamlit has been written. /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/real_time_token_monitoring_dashboard/dex_fetcher.py has been successfully created, with content:\nimport asyncio\nimport aiohttp\nfrom typing import Dict\n\nclass TokenData:\n    def __init__(self, name: str, contract_address: str, price: float, exchange: str):\n        self.name = name\n        self.contract_address = contract_address\n        self.price = price\n        self.exchange = exchange\n\nclass DEXFetcher:\n    def __init__(self, name: str, endpoint: str):\n        self.name = name\n        self.endpoint = endpoint\n\n    async def fetch_tokens(self) -> Dict[str, TokenData]:\n        """\n        Fetches token data from the DEX GraphQL endpoint.\n        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to TokenData.\n        """\n        # GraphQL queries for each DEX (Uniswap, PancakeSwap, Sushiswap)\n        # These queries fetch top tokens by liquidity and their price.\n        # For production, you may want to paginate or fetch more tokens.\n        query = """\n        {\n          tokens(first: 100, orderBy: volumeUSD, orderDirection: desc) {\n            id\n            symbol\n            name\n            derivedETH\n            derivedUSD\n          }\n        }\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "query": query\n        }\n        tokens = {}\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.post(self.endpoint, json=payload, headers=headers, timeout=15) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        print(f"[{self.name}] Error: Received status {resp.status}")\n                        return tokens\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    for token in data.get("data", {}).get("tokens", []):\n                        contract_address = token.get("id")\n                        name = token.get("name") or token.get("symbol") or contract_address\n                        # Prefer derivedUSD if available, else fallback to derivedETH (approximate with ETH price)\n                        price = None\n                        if token.get("derivedUSD") is not None:\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedUSD"])\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        elif token.get("derivedETH") is not None:\n                            # Fallback: Use ETH price from CoinGecko (could cache for efficiency)\n                            eth_price = await self._get_eth_price()\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedETH"]) * eth_price\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        if price is not None:\n                            tokens[contract_address] = TokenData(\n                                name=name,\n                                contract_address=contract_address,\n                                price=price,\n                                exchange=self.name\n                            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f"[{self.name}] Exception during fetch: {e}")\n        return tokens\n\n    async def _get_eth_price(self) -> float:\n        """\n        Fetches the current ETH price in USD from CoinGecko.\n        Used as a fallback if derivedUSD is not available.\n        """\n        url = "https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=ethereum&vs_currencies=usd"\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.get(url, timeout=10) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        return 0.0\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    return float(data.get("ethereum", {}).get("usd", 0.0))\n        except Exception:\n            return 0.0\n\n# Example DEX endpoints (mainnet)\nUNISWAP_V3_ENDPOINT = "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/uniswap/uniswap-v3"\nPANCAKESWAP_ENDPOINT = "https://bsc.streamingfast.io/subgraphs/name/pancakeswap/exchange-v2"\nSUSHISWAP_ENDPOINT = "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/sushiswap/exchange"\n\n# For testing purposes\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    async def main():\n        fetchers = [\n            DEXFetcher("Uniswap", UNISWAP_V3_ENDPOINT),\n            DEXFetcher("PancakeSwap", PANCAKESWAP_ENDPOINT),\n            DEXFetcher("Sushiswap", SUSHISWAP_ENDPOINT)\n        ]\n        results = await asyncio.gather(*(f.fetch_tokens() for f in fetchers))\n        for dex, tokens in zip(fetchers, results):\n            print(f"--- {dex.name} ---")\n            for t in list(tokens.values())[:5]:  # Print top 5 tokens for brevity\n                print(f"{t.name} ({t.contract_address}): ${t.price:.4f}")\n\n    asyncio.run(main())'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of TokenAnalyzer analyzes tokens from multiple DEXes, finds tokens available on at least two exchanges, and identifies arbitrage opportunities with price differences greater than a threshold."\n        }\n    }\n]\n```'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': 'Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/real_time_token_monitoring_dashboard/token_analyzer.py has been successfully created, with content:\nfrom typing import List, Dict, Optional\nfrom dex_fetcher import TokenData\n\nclass ArbitrageOpportunity:\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        token_name: str,\n        contract_address: str,\n        lowest_price: float,\n        highest_price: float,\n        lowest_exchange: str,\n        highest_exchange: str,\n        price_diff_percent: float,\n    ):\n        self.token_name = token_name\n        self.contract_address = contract_address\n        self.lowest_price = lowest_price\n        self.highest_price = highest_price\n        self.lowest_exchange = lowest_exchange\n        self.highest_exchange = highest_exchange\n        self.price_diff_percent = price_diff_percent\n\nclass TokenAnalyzer:\n    def __init__(self, dex_fetchers: List):\n        """\n        dex_fetchers: List of DEXFetcher instances\n        """\n        self.dex_fetchers = dex_fetchers\n\n    def get_common_tokens(\n        self, tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]]\n    ) -> Dict[str, List[TokenData]]:\n        """\n        Finds tokens available on at least two exchanges.\n        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to list of TokenData (from different exchanges).\n        """\n        token_map: Dict[str, List[TokenData]] = {}\n        for dex_tokens in tokens_by_dex:\n            for contract_address, token_data in dex_tokens.items():\n                if contract_address not in token_map:\n                    token_map[contract_address] = []\n                token_map[contract_address].append(token_data)\n        # Filter: only tokens present on at least two exchanges\n        common_tokens = {\n            k: v for k, v in token_map.items() if len(v) >= 2\n        }\n        return common_tokens\n\n    def find_arbitrage_opportunities(\n        self,\n        tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]],\n        threshold: float = 1.8,\n    ) -> List[ArbitrageOpportunity]:\n        """\n        Finds arbitrage opportunities for tokens available on at least two exchanges.\n        Returns a list of ArbitrageOpportunity objects where price difference > threshold (%).\n        """\n        opportunities: List[ArbitrageOpportunity] = []\n        common_tokens = self.get_common_tokens(tokens_by_dex)\n        for contract_address, token_datas in common_tokens.items():\n            # Find lowest and highest price and corresponding exchanges\n            lowest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            highest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            for td in token_datas:\n                if lowest is None or td.price < lowest.price:\n                    lowest = td\n                if highest is None or td.price > highest.price:\n                    highest = td\n            if lowest is None or highest is None or lowest.price == 0:\n                continue\n            price_diff_percent = ((highest.price - lowest.price) / lowest.price) * 100\n            if price_diff_percent > threshold:\n                opportunity = ArbitrageOpportunity(\n                    token_name=lowest.name,\n                    contract_address=contract_address,\n                    lowest_price=lowest.price,\n                    highest_price=highest.price,\n                    lowest_exchange=lowest.exchange,\n                    highest_exchange=highest.exchange,\n                    price_diff_percent=price_diff_percent,\n                )\n                opportunities.append(opportunity)\n        return opportunities'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of token_analyzer.py is complete, as the TokenAnalyzer, TokenData, and ArbitrageOpportunity classes have been written according to the system design. Calls fetch_and_analyze_callback to get arbitrage opportunities.\n        Updates every second.\n        """\n        st.set_page_config(page_title="Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard", layout="wide")\n        st.title("Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard")\n        st.caption("Monitoring Uniswap, PancakeSwap, and Sushiswap for arbitrage opportunities (refreshes every second)")\n\n        refresh_interval = 1  # seconds\n\n        # Streamlit\'s session state to persist data between reruns\n        if "last_update" not in st.session_state:\n            st.session_state["last_update"] = 0\n\n        # Main loop\n        while True:\n            start_time = time.time()\n            with st.spinner("Fetching latest token data and analyzing opportunities..."):\n                opportunities = fetch_and_analyze_callback()\n            st.subheader("Arbitrage Opportunities (>1.8% price difference)")\n            self.render_table(opportunities)\n            st.write(f"Last updated: {time.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\')}")\n            # Wait for the next refresh\n            elapsed = time.time() - start_time\n            if elapsed < refresh_interval:\n                time.sleep(refresh_interval - elapsed)\n            # Rerun Streamlit script\n            st.experimental_rerun()\n\n# Example usage (for main.py):\n# from token_analyzer import TokenAnalyzer\n# analyzer = TokenAnalyzer([...])\n# dashboard = Dashboard(analyzer)\n# dashboard.run(fetch_and_analyze_callback)'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of dashboard.py is complete, as the Dashboard class using Streamlit has been written. The system will consist of modular fetchers for each DEX, a token analyzer to compare prices and filter opportunities, and a dashboard component for display. Async programming (asyncio, aiohttp) will be used for efficient real-time updates. Error handling and rate limiting will be built in.\\n004|\\n005|## File list\\n006|\\n007|- main.py\\n008|- dex_fetcher.py\\n009|- token_analyzer.py\\n010|- dashboard.py\\n011|- config.py\\n012|- requirements.txt\\n013|- docs/system_design.md\\n014|\\n015|## Data structures and interfaces:\\n016|\\n017|```mermaid\\n018|classDiagram\\n019|    class DEXFetcher {\\n020|        +__init__(name: str, endpoint: str)\\n021|        +fetch_tokens() -> dict[str, TokenData]\\n022|    }\\n023|    class TokenData {\\n024|        +name: str\\n025|        +contract_address: str\\n026|        +price: float\\n027|        +exchange: str\\n028|    }\\n029|    class TokenAnalyzer {\\n030|        +__init__(dex_fetchers: list[DEXFetcher])\\n031|        +get_common_tokens() -> dict[str, list[TokenData]]\\n032|        +find_arbitrage_opportunities(threshold: float=1.8) -> list[ArbitrageOpportunity]\\n033|    }\\n034|    class ArbitrageOpportunity {\\n035|        +token_name: str\\n036|        +contract_address: str\\n037|        +lowest_price: float\\n038|        +highest_price: float\\n039|        +lowest_exchange: str\\n040|        +highest_exchange: str\\n041|        +price_diff_percent: float\\n042|    }\\n043|    class Dashboard {\\n044|        +__init__(analyzer: TokenAnalyzer)\\n045|        +render_table(opportunities: list[ArbitrageOpportunity])\\n046|        +run()\\n047|    }\\n048|    DEXFetcher <.. def fetch_tokens(self) -> Dict[str, TokenData]:\n        """\n        Fetches token data from the DEX GraphQL endpoint.\n        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to TokenData.\n        """\n        # GraphQL queries for each DEX (Uniswap, PancakeSwap, Sushiswap)\n        # These queries fetch top tokens by liquidity and their price.\n        # For production, you may want to paginate or fetch more tokens.\n        query = """\n        {\n          tokens(first: 100, orderBy: volumeUSD, orderDirection: desc) {\n            id\n            symbol\n            name\n            derivedETH\n            derivedUSD\n          }\n        }\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "query": query\n        }\n        tokens = {}\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.post(self.endpoint, json=payload, headers=headers, timeout=15) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        print(f"[{self.name}] Error: Received status {resp.status}")\n                        return tokens\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    for token in data.get("data", {}).get("tokens", []):\n                        contract_address = token.get("id")\n                        name = token.get("name") or token.get("symbol") or contract_address\n                        # Prefer derivedUSD if available, else fallback to derivedETH (approximate with ETH price)\n                        price = None\n                        if token.get("derivedUSD") is not None:\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedUSD"])\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        elif token.get("derivedETH") is not None:\n                            # Fallback: Use ETH price from        tokens[contract_address] = TokenData(\n                                name=name,\n                                contract_address=contract_address,\n                                price=price,\n                                exchange=self.name\n                            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f"[{self.name}] Exception during fetch: {e}")\n        return tokens\n\n    async def _get_eth_price(self) -> float:\n        """\n        Fetches the current ETH price in USD from CoinGecko.\n        Used as a fallback if derivedUSD is not available.\n        """\n        url = "https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=ethereum&vs_currencies=usd"\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.get(url, timeout=10) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        return 0.0\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    return Filter: only tokens present on at least two exchanges\n        common_tokens = {\n            k: v for k, v in token_map.items() if len(v) >= 2\n        }\n        return common_tokens\n\n    def find_arbitrage_opportunities(\n        self,\n        tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]],\n        threshold: float = 1.8,\n    ) -> List[ArbitrageOpportunity]:\n        """\n        Finds arbitrage opportunities for tokens available on at least two exchanges.\n        Returns a list of ArbitrageOpportunity objects where price difference > threshold (%).\n        """\n        opportunities: List[ArbitrageOpportunity] = []\n        common_tokens = self.get_common_tokens(tokens_by_dex)\n        for contract_address, token_datas in common_tokens.items():\n            # Find lowest and highest price and corresponding exchanges\n            lowest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            highest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            for td in token_datas:\n                if lowest is None or td.price < lowest.price:\n                    lowest = td\n                if highest is None or td.price > highest.price:\n                    highest = td\n            if lowest is None or highest is None or lowest.price == 0:\n                continue\n            price_diff_percent = ((highest.price - lowest.price) / lowest.price) * 100\n            if price_diff_percent > threshold:\n                opportunity = ArbitrageOpportunity(\n                    token_name=lowest.name,\n                    contract_address=contract_address,\n                    lowest_price=lowest.price,\n                    highest_price=highest.price,\n                    lowest_exchange=lowest.exchange,\n                    highest_exchange=highest.exchange,\n                    price_diff_percent=price_diff_percent,\n                )\n                opportunities.append(opportunity)\n        return opportunities'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of token_analyzer.py is complete, as the TokenAnalyzer, TokenData, and ArbitrageOpportunity classes have been written according to the system design. Calls fetch_and_analyze_callback to get arbitrage opportunities.\n        Updates every second.\n        """\n        st.set_page_config(page_title="Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard", layout="wide")\n        st.title("Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard")\n        st.caption("Monitoring Uniswap, PancakeSwap, and Sushiswap for arbitrage opportunities (refreshes every second)")\n\n        refresh_interval = 1  # seconds\n\n        # Streamlit\'s session state to persist data between reruns\n        if "last_update" not in st.session_state:\n            st.session_state["last_update"] = 0\n\n        # Main loop\n        while True:\n            start_time = time.time()\n            with st.spinner("Fetching latest token data and analyzing opportunities..."):\n                opportunities = fetch_and_analyze_callback()\n            st.subheader("Arbitrage Opportunities (>1.8% price difference)")\n            self.render_table(opportunities)\n            st.write(f"Last updated: {time.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\')}")\n            # Wait for the next refresh\n            elapsed = time.time() - start_time\n            if elapsed < refresh_interval:\n                time.sleep(refresh_interval - elapsed)\n            # Rerun Streamlit script\n            st.experimental_rerun()\n\n# Example usage (for main.py):\n# from token_analyzer import TokenAnalyzer\n# analyzer = TokenAnalyzer([...])\n# dashboard = Dashboard(analyzer)\n# dashboard.run(fetch_and_analyze_callback)'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of dashboard.py is complete, as the Dashboard class using Streamlit has been written. # config.py
# Configuration for real_time_token_monitoring_dashboard

# DEX GraphQL endpoints
UNISWAP_V3_ENDPOINT = "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/uniswap/uniswap-v3"
PANCAKESWAP_ENDPOINT = "https://bsc.streamingfast.io/subgraphs/name/pancakeswap/exchange-v2"
SUSHISWAP_ENDPOINT = "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/sushiswap/exchange"

DEX_CONFIGS = [
    {"name": "Uniswap", "endpoint": UNISWAP_V3_ENDPOINT},
    {"name": "PancakeSwap", "endpoint": PANCAKESWAP_ENDPOINT},
    {"name": "Sushiswap", "endpoint": SUSHISWAP_ENDPOINT},
]

# Arbitrage threshold in percent
ARBITRAGE_THRESHOLD = 1.8

# Number of tokens to fetch per DEX (can be tuned for performance)
TOKENS_FETCH_LIMIT = 100

# GraphQL query for fetching tokens (used in dex_fetcher.py)
GRAPHQL_TOKEN_QUERY = """
{
  tokens(first: %d, orderBy: volumeUSD, orderDirection: desc) {
    id
    symbol
    name
    derivedETH
    derivedUSD
  }
}
""" % TOKENS_FETCH_LIMIT

# CoinGecko API endpoint for ETH price (used as fallback in dex_fetcher.py)
COINGECKO_ETH_PRICE_URL = "https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=ethereum&vs_currencies=usd"

# Dashboard settings
DASHBOARD_TITLE = "Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard"
DASHBOARD_REFRESH_INTERVAL = 1  # seconds

# Error handling
API_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 15
API_RETRY_COUNT = 2

# You can add more configuration options as needed for future features.

Dict\n\nclass TokenData:\n    def __init__(self, name: str, contract_address: str, price: float, exchange: str):\n        self.name = name\n        self.contract_address = contract_address\n def fetch_tokens(self) -> Dict[str, TokenData]:\n        """\n        Fetches token data from the DEX GraphQL endpoint.\n        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to TokenData.\n        """\n        # GraphQL queries for each DEX (Uniswap, PancakeSwap, Sushiswap)\n        # These queries fetch top tokens by liquidity and their price.\n        # For production, you may want to paginate or fetch more tokens.\n        query = """\n        {\n          tokens(first: 100, orderBy: volumeUSD, orderDirection: desc) {\n            id\n            symbol\n            name\n            derivedETH\n            derivedUSD\n          }\n        }\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "query": query\n        }\n        tokens = {}\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.post(self.endpoint, json=payload, headers=headers, timeout=15) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        print(f"[{self.name}] Error: Received status {resp.status}")\n                        return tokens\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    for token in data.get("data", {}).get("tokens", []):\n                        contract_address = token.get("id")\n                        name = token.get("name") or token.get("symbol") or contract_address\n                        # Prefer derivedUSD if available, else fallback to derivedETH (approximate with ETH price)\n                        price = None\n                        if token.get("derivedUSD") is not None:\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedUSD"])\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        elif token.get("derivedETH") is not None:\n                            # Fallback: Use ETH price from CoinGecko (could cache for efficiency)\n                            eth_price = await self._get_eth_price()\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedETH"]) * eth_price\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        if price is not None:\n                            tokens[contract_address] = TokenData(\n                                name=name,\n                                contract_address=contract_address,\n                                price=price,\n                                exchange=self.name\n                            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f"[{self.name}] Exception during fetch: {e}")\n        return tokens\n\n    async def _get_eth_price(self) -> float:\n        """\n        Fetches the current ETH price in USD from CoinGecko.\n        Used as a fallback if derivedUSD is not available.\n        """\n        url = "https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=ethereum&vs_currencies=usd"\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.get(url, timeout=10) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        return 0.0\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    return float(data.get("ethereum", {}).get("usd", 0.0))\n        except Exception:\n            return 0.0\n\n# Example DEX endpoints (mainnet)\nUNISWAP_V3_ENDPOINT = Filter: only tokens present on at least two exchanges\n        common_tokens = {\n            k: v for k, v in token_map.items() if len(v) >= 2\n        }\n        return common_tokens\n\n    def find_arbitrage_opportunities(\n        self,\n        tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]],\n        threshold: float = 1.8,\n    ) -> List[ArbitrageOpportunity]:\n        """\n        Finds arbitrage opportunities for tokens available on at least two exchanges.\n        Returns a list of ArbitrageOpportunity objects where price difference > threshold (%).\n        """\n        opportunities: List[ArbitrageOpportunity] = []\n        common_tokens = self.get_common_tokens(tokens_by_dex)\n        for contract_address, token_datas in common_tokens.items():\n            # Find lowest and highest price and corresponding exchanges\n            lowest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            highest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            for td in token_datas:\n                if lowest is None or td.price < lowest.price:\n                    lowest = td\n                if highest is None or td.price > highest.price:\n                    highest = td\n            if lowest is None or highest is None or lowest.price == 0:\n                continue\n            price_diff_percent = ((highest.price - lowest.price) / lowest.price) * 100\n            if price_diff_percent > threshold:\n                opportunity = ArbitrageOpportunity(\n                    token_name=lowest.name,\n                    contract_address=contract_address,\n                    lowest_price=lowest.price,\n                    highest_price=highest.price,\n                    lowest_exchange=lowest.exchange,\n                    highest_exchange=highest.exchange,\n                    price_diff_percent=price_diff_percent,\n                )\n                opportunities.append(opportunity)\n        return opportunities'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of token_analyzer.py is complete, as the TokenAnalyzer, TokenData, and ArbitrageOpportunity classes have been written according to the system design. Calls fetch_and_analyze_callback to get arbitrage opportunities.\n        Updates every second.\n        """\n        st.set_page_config(page_title="Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard", layout="wide")\n        st.title("Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard")\n        st.caption("Monitoring Uniswap, PancakeSwap, and Sushiswap for arbitrage opportunities (refreshes every second)")\n\n        refresh_interval = 1  # seconds\n\n        # Streamlit\'s session state to persist data between reruns\n        if "last_update" not in st.session_state:\n            st.session_state["last_update"] = 0\n\n        # Main loop\n        while True:\n            start_time = time.time()\n            with st.spinner("Fetching latest token data and analyzing opportunities..."):\n                opportunities = fetch_and_analyze_callback()\n            st.subheader("Arbitrage Opportunities (>1.8% price difference)")\n            self.render_table(opportunities)\n            st.write(f"Last updated: {time.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\')}")\n            # Wait for the next refresh\n            elapsed = time.time() - start_time\n            if elapsed < refresh_interval:\n                time.sleep(refresh_interval - elapsed)\n            # Rerun Streamlit script\n            st.experimental_rerun()\n\n# Example usage (for main.py):\n# from token_analyzer import TokenAnalyzer\n# analyzer = TokenAnalyzer([...])\n# dashboard = Dashboard(analyzer)\n# dashboard.run(fetch_and_analyze_callback)'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of dashboard.py is complete, as the Dashboard class using Streamlit has been written. Dict\n\nclass TokenData:\n    def __init__(self, name: str, contract_address: str, price: float, exchange: str):\n        self.name = name\n        self.contract_address = contract_address\n        self.price = price\n        self.exchange = exchange\n\nclass DEXFetcher:\n    def __init__(self, name: str, endpoint: str):\n        self.name = name\n        self.endpoint = endpoint\n\n    async def fetch_tokens(self) -> Dict[str, TokenData]:\n        """\n        Fetches token data from the DEX GraphQL endpoint.\n        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to TokenData.\n        """\n        # GraphQL queries for each DEX (Uniswap, PancakeSwap, Sushiswap)\n        # These queries fetch top tokens by liquidity and their price.\n        # For production, you may want to paginate or fetch more tokens.\n        query = """\n        {\n          tokens(first: 100, orderBy: volumeUSD, orderDirection: desc) {\n            id\n            symbol\n            name\n            derivedETH\n            derivedUSD\n          }\n        }\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "query": query\n        }\n        tokens = {}\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.post(self.endpoint, json=payload, headers=headers, timeout=15) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        print(f"[{self.name}] Error: Received status {resp.status}")\n                        return tokens\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    for token in data.get("data", {}).get("tokens", []):\n                        contract_address = token.get("id")\n                        name = token.get("name") or token.get("symbol") or contract_address\n                        # Prefer derivedUSD if available, else fallback to derivedETH (approximate with ETH price)\n                        price = None\n                        if token.get("derivedUSD") is not None:\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedUSD"])\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        elif token.get("derivedETH") is not None:\n                            # Fallback: Use ETH price from CoinGecko (could cache for efficiency)\n                            eth_price = await self._get_eth_price()\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedETH"]) * eth_price\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        if price is not None:\n                            tokens[contract_address] = TokenData(\n                                name=name,\n                                contract_address=contract_address,\n                                price=price,\n                                exchange=self.name\n                            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f"[{self.name}] Exception during fetch: {e}")\n        return tokens\n\n    async def _get_eth_price(self) -> float:\n        """\n        Fetches the current ETH price in USD from CoinGecko.\n        Used as a fallback if derivedUSD is not available.\n        """\n        url = "https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=ethereum&vs_currencies=usd"\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.get(url, timeout=10) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        return 0.0\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    return float(data.get("ethereum", {}).get("usd", 0.0))\n        except Exception:\n            return 0.0\n\n# Example DEX endpoints (mainnet)\nUNISWAP_V3_ENDPOINT = asyncio.gather(*(f.fetch_tokens() for f in fetchers))\n        for dex, tokens in zip(fetchers, results):\n            print(f"--- {dex.name} ---")\n            for t in list(tokens.values())[:5]:  # TokenAnalyzer analyzes tokens from multiple DEXes, finds tokens available on at least two exchanges, and identifies arbitrage opportunities with price differences greater than a threshold."\n        }\n    }\n]\n```'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': 'Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/real_time_token_monitoring_dashboard/token_analyzer.py has been successfully created, with content:\nfrom typing import List, Dict, Optional\nfrom dex_fetcher import TokenData\n\nclass ArbitrageOpportunity:\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        token_name: str,\n        contract_address: str,\n        lowest_price: float,\n        highest_price: float,\n        lowest_exchange: str,\n        highest_exchange: str,\n        price_diff_percent: float,\n    ):\n        self.token_name = token_name\n        self.contract_address = contract_address\n        self.lowest_price = lowest_price\n        self.highest_price = highest_price\n        self.lowest_exchange = lowest_exchange\n        self.highest_exchange = highest_exchange\n        self.price_diff_percent = price_diff_percent\n\nclass TokenAnalyzer:\n    def __init__(self, dex_fetchers: List):\n        """\n        dex_fetchers: List of DEXFetcher instances\n        """\n        self.dex_fetchers = dex_fetchers\n\n    def get_common_tokens(\n        self, tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]]\n    ) -> Dict[str, List[TokenData]]:\n        """\n        Finds tokens available on at least two exchanges.\n        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to list of TokenData (from different exchanges).\n        """\n        token_map: Dict[str, List[TokenData]] = {}\n        for dex_tokens in tokens_by_dex:\n            for contract_address, token_data in dex_tokens.items():\n                if contract_address not in token_map:\n                    token_map[contract_address] = []\n                token_map[contract_address].append(token_data)\n        # Filter: only tokens present on at least two exchanges\n        common_tokens = {\n            k: v for k, v in token_map.items() if len(v) >= 2\n        }\n        return common_tokens\n\n    def find_arbitrage_opportunities(\n        self,\n        tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]],\n        threshold: float = 1.8,\n    ) -> List[ArbitrageOpportunity]:\n        """\n        Finds arbitrage opportunities for tokens available on at least two exchanges.\n        Returns a list of ArbitrageOpportunity objects where price difference > threshold (%).\n        """\n        opportunities: List[ArbitrageOpportunity] = []\n        common_tokens = self.get_common_tokens(tokens_by_dex)\n        for contract_address, token_datas in common_tokens.items():\n            # Find lowest and highest price and corresponding exchanges\n            lowest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            highest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            for td in token_datas:\n                if lowest is None or td.price < lowest.price:\n                    lowest = td\n                if highest is None or td.price > highest.price:\n                    highest = td\n            if lowest is None or highest is None or lowest.price == 0:\n                continue\n            price_diff_percent = ((highest.price - lowest.price) / lowest.price) * 100\n            if price_diff_percent > threshold:\n                opportunity = ArbitrageOpportunity(\n                    token_name=lowest.name,\n                    contract_address=contract_address,\n                    lowest_price=lowest.price,\n                    highest_price=highest.price,\n                    lowest_exchange=lowest.exchange,\n                    highest_exchange=highest.exchange,\n                    price_diff_percent=price_diff_percent,\n                )\n                opportunities.append(opportunity)\n        return opportunities'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of token_analyzer.py is complete, as the TokenAnalyzer, TokenData, and ArbitrageOpportunity classes have been written according to the system design. Calls fetch_and_analyze_callback to get arbitrage opportunities.\n        Updates every second.\n        """\n        st.set_page_config(page_title="Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard", layout="wide")\n        st.title("Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard")\n        st.caption("Monitoring Uniswap, PancakeSwap, and Sushiswap for arbitrage opportunities (refreshes every second)")\n\n        refresh_interval = 1  # seconds\n\n        # Streamlit\'s session state to persist data between reruns\n        if "last_update" not in st.session_state:\n            st.session_state["last_update"] = 0\n\n        # Main loop\n        while True:\n            start_time = time.time()\n            with st.spinner("Fetching latest token data and analyzing opportunities..."):\n                opportunities = fetch_and_analyze_callback()\n            st.subheader("Arbitrage Opportunities (>1.8% price difference)")\n            self.render_table(opportunities)\n            st.write(f"Last updated: {time.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\')}")\n            # Wait for the next refresh\n            elapsed = time.time() - start_time\n            if elapsed < refresh_interval:\n                time.sleep(refresh_interval - elapsed)\n            # Rerun Streamlit script\n            st.experimental_rerun()\n\n# Example usage (for main.py):\n# from token_analyzer import TokenAnalyzer\n# analyzer = TokenAnalyzer([...])\n# dashboard = Dashboard(analyzer)\n# dashboard.run(fetch_and_analyze_callback)'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of dashboard.py is complete, as the Dashboard class using Streamlit has been written.     # GraphQL queries for each DEX (Uniswap, PancakeSwap, Sushiswap)\n        # These queries fetch top tokens by liquidity and their price.\n        # For production, you may want to paginate or fetch more tokens.\n        query = """\n        {\n          tokens(first: 100, orderBy: volumeUSD, orderDirection: desc) {\n            id\n            symbol\n            name\n            derivedETH\n            derivedUSD\n          }\n        }\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "query": query\n        }\n        tokens = {}\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.post(self.endpoint, json=payload, headers=headers, timeout=15) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        print(f"[{self.name}] Error: Received status {resp.status}")\n                        return tokens\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    for token in data.get("data", {}).get("tokens", []):\n                        contract_address = token.get("id")\n                        name = token.get("name") or token.get("symbol") or contract_address\n                        # Prefer derivedUSD if available, else fallback to derivedETH (approximate with ETH price)\n                        price = None\n                        if token.get("derivedUSD") is not None:\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedUSD"])\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        elif token.get("derivedETH") is not None:\n                            # Fallback: Use ETH price from        tokens[contract_address] = TokenData(\n                                name=name,\n                                contract_address=contract_address,\n                                price=price,\n                                exchange=self.name\n                            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f"[{self.name}] Exception during fetch: {e}")\n        return tokens\n\n    async def _get_eth_price(self) -> float:\n        """\n        Fetches the current ETH price in USD from CoinGecko.\n        Used as a fallback if derivedUSD is not available.\n        """\n        url = "https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=ethereum&vs_currencies=usd"\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.get(url, timeout=10) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        return 0.0\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    return Filter: only tokens present on at least two exchanges\n        common_tokens = {\n            k: v for k, v in token_map.items() if len(v) >= 2\n        }\n        return common_tokens\n\n    def find_arbitrage_opportunities(\n        self,\n        tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]],\n        threshold: float = 1.8,\n    ) -> List[ArbitrageOpportunity]:\n        """\n        Finds arbitrage opportunities for tokens available on at least two exchanges.\n        Returns a list of ArbitrageOpportunity objects where price difference > threshold (%).\n        """\n        opportunities: List[ArbitrageOpportunity] = []\n        common_tokens = self.get_common_tokens(tokens_by_dex)\n        for contract_address, token_datas in common_tokens.items():\n            # Find lowest and highest price and corresponding exchanges\n            lowest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            highest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            for td in token_datas:\n                if lowest is None or td.price < lowest.price:\n                    lowest = td\n                if highest is None or td.price > highest.price:\n                    highest = td\n            if lowest is None or highest is None or lowest.price == 0:\n                continue\n            price_diff_percent = ((highest.price - lowest.price) / lowest.price) * 100\n            if price_diff_percent > threshold:\n                opportunity = ArbitrageOpportunity(\n                    token_name=lowest.name,\n                    contract_address=contract_address,\n                    lowest_price=lowest.price,\n                    highest_price=highest.price,\n                    lowest_exchange=lowest.exchange,\n                    highest_exchange=highest.exchange,\n                    price_diff_percent=price_diff_percent,\n                )\n                opportunities.append(opportunity)\n        return opportunities'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of token_analyzer.py is complete, as the TokenAnalyzer, TokenData, and ArbitrageOpportunity classes have been written according to the system design. Calls fetch_and_analyze_callback to get arbitrage opportunities.\n        Updates every second.\n        """\n        st.set_page_config(page_title="Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard", layout="wide")\n        st.title("Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard")\n        st.caption("Monitoring Uniswap, PancakeSwap, and Sushiswap for arbitrage opportunities (refreshes every second)")\n\n        refresh_interval = 1  # seconds\n\n        # Streamlit\'s session state to persist data between reruns\n        if "last_update" not in st.session_state:\n            st.session_state["last_update"] = 0\n\n        # Main loop\n        while True:\n            start_time = time.time()\n            with st.spinner("Fetching latest token data and analyzing opportunities..."):\n                opportunities = fetch_and_analyze_callback()\n            st.subheader("Arbitrage Opportunities (>1.8% price difference)")\n            self.render_table(opportunities)\n            st.write(f"Last updated: {time.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\')}")\n            # Wait for the next refresh\n            elapsed = time.time() - start_time\n            if elapsed < refresh_interval:\n                time.sleep(refresh_interval - elapsed)\n            # Rerun Streamlit script\n            st.experimental_rerun()\n\n# Example usage (for main.py):\n# from token_analyzer import TokenAnalyzer\n# analyzer = TokenAnalyzer([...])\n# dashboard = Dashboard(analyzer)\n# dashboard.run(fetch_and_analyze_callback)'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of dashboard.py is complete, as the Dashboard class using Streamlit has been written. Run async fetching in a synchronous context for Streamlit\n        loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()\n        asyncio.set_event_loop(loop)\n        tokens_by_dex = loop.run_until_complete(fetch_all_tokens(dex_fetchers))\n        opportunities = analyzer.find_arbitrage_opportunities(tokens_by_dex, threshold=ARBITRAGE_THRESHOLD)\n        return opportunities\n    return fetch_and_analyze_callback\n\ndef main():\n    dex_fetchers = create_dex_fetchers()\n    analyzer = TokenAnalyzer(dex_fetchers)\n    dashboard = Dashboard(analyzer)\n    fetch_and_analyze_callback = fetch_and_analyze_callback_factory(dex_fetchers, analyzer)\n    dashboard.run(fetch_and_analyze_callback)\n\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    main()'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of main.py is complete, as the file has been successfully created and matches the requirements of orchestrating the fetching, analysis, and dashboard update loop. def fetch_tokens(self) -> Dict[str, TokenData]:\n        """\n        Fetches token data from the DEX GraphQL endpoint.\n        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to TokenData.\n        """\n        # GraphQL queries for each DEX (Uniswap, PancakeSwap, Sushiswap)\n        # These queries fetch top tokens by liquidity and their price.\n        # For production, you may want to paginate or derivedETH\n            derivedUSD\n          }\n        }\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "query": query\n        }\n        tokens = {}\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.post(self.endpoint, json=payload, headers=headers, token.get("name") or token.get("symbol") or contract_address\n                        # Prefer derivedUSD if available, else fallback to derivedETH (approximate with ETH price)\n                        price = None\n                        if token.get("derivedUSD") is not None:\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedUSD"])\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        elif token.get("derivedETH") is not None:\n                            # Fallback: Use ETH price from price=price,\n                                exchange=self.name\n                            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f"[{self.name}] Exception during fetch: {e}")\n        return tokens\n\n    async def _get_eth_price(self) -> float:\n        """\n        Fetches the current ETH price in USD from CoinGecko.\n        Used as a fallback if derivedUSD is not available.\n        """\n        url = "https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=ethereum&vs_currencies=usd"\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.get(url, timeout=10) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        return 0.0\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    return Filter: only tokens present on at least two exchanges\n        common_tokens = {\n            k: v for k, v in token_map.items() if len(v) >= 2\n        }\n        return common_tokens\n\n    def find_arbitrage_opportunities(\n        self,\n        tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]],\n        threshold: float = 1.8,\n    ) -> List[ArbitrageOpportunity]:\n        """\n        Finds arbitrage opportunities for tokens available on at least two exchanges.\n        Returns a list of ArbitrageOpportunity objects where price difference > threshold (%).\n        """\n        opportunities: List[ArbitrageOpportunity] = []\n        common_tokens = self.get_common_tokens(tokens_by_dex)\n        for contract_address, token_datas in common_tokens.items():\n            # Find lowest and highest price and corresponding exchanges\n            lowest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            highest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            for td in token_datas:\n                if lowest is None or td.price < lowest.price:\n                    lowest = td\n                if highest is None or td.price > highest.price:\n                    highest = td\n            if lowest is None or highest is None or lowest.price == 0:\n                continue\n            price_diff_percent = ((highest.price - lowest.price) / lowest.price) * 100\n            if price_diff_percent > threshold:\n                opportunity = ArbitrageOpportunity(\n                    token_name=lowest.name,\n                    contract_address=contract_address,\n                    lowest_price=lowest.price,\n                    highest_price=highest.price,\n                    lowest_exchange=lowest.exchange,\n                    highest_exchange=highest.exchange,\n                    price_diff_percent=price_diff_percent,\n                )\n                opportunities.append(opportunity)\n        return opportunities'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of token_analyzer.py is complete, as the TokenAnalyzer, TokenData, and ArbitrageOpportunity classes have been written according to the system design. Calls fetch_and_analyze_callback to get arbitrage opportunities.\n        Updates every second.\n        """\n        st.set_page_config(page_title="Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard", layout="wide")\n        st.title("Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard")\n        st.caption("Monitoring Uniswap, PancakeSwap, and Sushiswap for arbitrage opportunities (refreshes every second)")\n\n        refresh_interval = 1  # seconds\n\n        # Streamlit\'s session state to persist data between reruns\n        if "last_update" not in st.session_state:\n            st.session_state["last_update"] = 0\n\n        # Main loop\n        while True:\n            start_time = time.time()\n            with st.spinner("Fetching latest token data and analyzing opportunities..."):\n                opportunities = fetch_and_analyze_callback()\n            st.subheader("Arbitrage Opportunities (>1.8% price difference)")\n            self.render_table(opportunities)\n            st.write(f"Last updated: {time.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\')}")\n            # Wait for the next refresh\n            elapsed = time.time() - start_time\n            if elapsed < refresh_interval:\n                time.sleep(refresh_interval - elapsed)\n            # Rerun Streamlit script\n            st.experimental_rerun()\n\n# Example usage (for main.py):\n# from token_analyzer import TokenAnalyzer\n# analyzer = TokenAnalyzer([...])\n# dashboard = Dashboard(analyzer)\n# dashboard.run(fetch_and_analyze_callback)'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of dashboard.py is complete, as the Dashboard class using Streamlit has been written. Deliver a user-friendly dashboard that updates every second with actionable token data.\\n015|\\n016|### 2.2 User Stories\\n017|- As a crypto trader, I want to see tokens listed on multiple DEXs so that I can identify arbitrage opportunities.\\n018|- As a DeFi analyst, I want to monitor real-time price differences across exchanges so that I can make informed trading decisions.\\n019|- As a developer, I want a dashboard that updates every second so that I always have the latest data.\\n020|- As a user, I want to see token names, contract addresses, and which exchanges have the lowest and highest prices so that I can act quickly.\\n021|\\n022|### 2.3 Competitive Analysis\\n023|| Product                | Pros                                              | Cons                                      |\\n024||------------------------|--------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------|\\n025|| DexTools               | Real-time data, multi-DEX support                 | Limited customization, may lag on updates |\\n026|| GeckoTerminal          | Clean UI, supports many DEXs                      | Not open-source, update interval unclear  |\\n027|| DexScreener            | Fast updates, good filtering                      | Limited to listed DEXs, not programmable  |\\n028|| Uniswap Analytics      | Deep Uniswap data                                 | Single DEX only                           |\\n029|| PancakeSwap Analytics  | Deep PancakeSwap data                             | Single DEX only                           |\\n030|| Sushiswap Analytics    | Deep Sushiswap data                               | Single DEX only                           |\\n031|| Custom Trading Bots    | Fully customizable, real-time                     | Requires development, no UI by default    |\\n032|\\n033|### 2.4 Competitive Quadrant Chart\\n034|```mermaid\\n035|quadrantChart\\n036|    title "DEX Token Monitoring Tools"\\n037|    x-axis "Low Customization" --> "High Customization"\\n038|    y-axis "Low Real-Time" --> "High Real-Time"\\n039|    quadrant-1 "Niche Tools"\\n040|    quadrant-2 "General Tools"\\n041|    quadrant-3 "Basic 0.4]\\n047|    "PancakeSwap Analytics": [0.1, 0.4]\\n048|    "Sushiswap Analytics": [0.1, 0.4]\\n049|    "Custom Trading Bots": [0.9, 0.9]\\n050|    "Our Target Product": [0.8, 0.95]\\n051|```\\n052|\\n053|## 3. Technical Specifications\\n054|### 3.1 Requirements Analysis\\n055|- Must fetch token price and metadata from Uniswap, PancakeSwap, and Sushiswap APIs or subgraphs.\\n056|- Must identify tokens available on at least two exchanges.\\n057|- Must calculate price differences and filter for those >1.8%.\\n058|- Must display token name, contract address, lowest/highest price exchanges, and price difference.\\n059|- Must update dashboard every second.\\n060|- Should handle API rate limits and errors gracefully.\\n061|- May allow user to filter or sort results.\\n062|\\n063|### 3.2 Requirements Pool\\n064|- **P0:** Real-time data fetching from all three DEXs\\n065|- **P0:** Identify tokens on at least two exchanges\\n066|- **P0:** Calculate and display price differences >1.8%\\n067|- **P0:** Dashboard UI with 1-second refresh\\n068|- **P1:** Error handling and fallback for API issues\\n069|- **P1:** User filtering/sorting options\\n070|- **P2:** Historical price charting\\n071|\\n072|### 3.3 UI Design Draft\\n073|- Table view with columns: Token Name | Contract Address | Lowest Price Exchange | Highest Price Exchange | Price Difference (%)\\n074|- Real-time update indicator\\n075|- Optional: Filter/search bar\\n076|\\n077|### 3.4 Open Questions\\n078|- What is the preferred dashboard framework (Dash, Streamlit, custom Flask app)?\\n079|- Should the dashboard support mobile devices?\\n080|- Is there a need for user authentication or public access?\\n081|- Should historical data be stored for later analysis?\\n\''}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'I have finished reading the PRD, which provides a clear overview of the requirements and technical specifications. Dict\n\nclass TokenData:\n    def __init__(self, name: str, contract_address: str, price: float, exchange: str):\n        self.name = name\n        self.contract_address = contract_address\n        self.price = price\n        self.exchange = exchange\n\nclass DEXFetcher:\n    def __init__(self, name: str, endpoint: str):\n        self.name = name\n        self.endpoint = endpoint\n\n    async def fetch_tokens(self) -> Dict[str, TokenData]:\n        """\n        Fetches token data from the DEX GraphQL endpoint.\n        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to TokenData.\n        """\n        # GraphQL queries for each DEX (Uniswap, PancakeSwap, Sushiswap)\n        # These queries fetch top tokens by liquidity and their price.\n        # For production, you may want to paginate or fetch more tokens.\n        query = """\n        {\n          tokens(first: 100, orderBy: volumeUSD, orderDirection: desc) {\n            id\n            symbol\n            name\n            derivedETH\n            derivedUSD\n          }\n        }\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "query": query\n        }\n        tokens = {}\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.post(self.endpoint, json=payload, headers=headers, timeout=15) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        print(f"[{self.name}] Error: Received status {resp.status}")\n                        return tokens\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    for token in data.get("data", {}).get("tokens", []):\n                        contract_address = token.get("id")\n                        name = token.get("name") or token.get("symbol") or contract_address\n                        # Prefer derivedUSD if available, else fallback to derivedETH (approximate with ETH price)\n                        price = None\n                        if token.get("derivedUSD") is not None:\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedUSD"])\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        elif token.get("derivedETH") is not None:\n                            # Fallback: Use ETH price from CoinGecko (could cache for efficiency)\n                            eth_price = await self._get_eth_price()\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedETH"]) * eth_price\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        if price is not None:\n                            tokens[contract_address] = TokenData(\n                                name=name,\n                                contract_address=contract_address,\n                                price=price,\n                                exchange=self.name\n                            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f"[{self.name}] Exception during fetch: {e}")\n        return tokens\n\n    async def _get_eth_price(self) -> float:\n        """\n        Fetches the current ETH price in USD from CoinGecko.\n        Used as a fallback if derivedUSD is not available.\n        """\n        url = "https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=ethereum&vs_currencies=usd"\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.get(url, timeout=10) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        return 0.0\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    return float(data.get("ethereum", {}).get("usd", 0.0))\n        except Exception:\n            return 0.0\n\n# Example DEX endpoints (mainnet)\nUNISWAP_V3_ENDPOINT = "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/uniswap/uniswap-v3"\nPANCAKESWAP_ENDPOINT = "https://bsc.streamingfast.io/subgraphs/name/pancakeswap/exchange-v2"\nSUSHISWAP_ENDPOINT = "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/sushiswap/exchange"\n\n# For testing purposes\nif __name__ == "__main__":\n    async def main():\n        fetchers = [\n            DEXFetcher("Uniswap", UNISWAP_V3_ENDPOINT),\n            DEXFetcher("PancakeSwap", PANCAKESWAP_ENDPOINT),\n            DEXFetcher("Sushiswap", SUSHISWAP_ENDPOINT)\n        ]\n        results = await asyncio.gather(*(f.fetch_tokens() for f in fetchers))\n        for dex, tokens in zip(fetchers, results):\n            print(f"--- {dex.name} ---")\n            for t in list(tokens.values())[:5]:  # TokenAnalyzer analyzes tokens from multiple DEXes, finds tokens available on at least two exchanges, and identifies arbitrage opportunities with price differences greater than a threshold."\n        }\n    }\n]\n```'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': 'Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/real_time_token_monitoring_dashboard/token_analyzer.py has been successfully created, with content:\nfrom typing import List, Dict, Optional\nfrom dex_fetcher import TokenData\n\nclass ArbitrageOpportunity:\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        token_name: str,\n        contract_address: str,\n        lowest_price: float,\n        highest_price: float,\n        lowest_exchange: str,\n        highest_exchange: str,\n        price_diff_percent: float,\n    ):\n        self.token_name = token_name\n        self.contract_address = contract_address\n        self.lowest_price = lowest_price\n        self.highest_price = highest_price\n        self.lowest_exchange = lowest_exchange\n        self.highest_exchange = highest_exchange\n        self.price_diff_percent = price_diff_percent\n\nclass TokenAnalyzer:\n    def __init__(self, dex_fetchers: List):\n        """\n        dex_fetchers: List of DEXFetcher instances\n        """\n        self.dex_fetchers = dex_fetchers\n\n    def get_common_tokens(\n        self, tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]]\n    ) -> Dict[str, List[TokenData]]:\n        """\n        Finds tokens available on at least two exchanges.\n        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to list of TokenData (from different exchanges).\n        """\n        token_map: Dict[str, List[TokenData]] = {}\n        for dex_tokens in tokens_by_dex:\n            for contract_address, token_data in dex_tokens.items():\n                if contract_address not in token_map:\n                    token_map[contract_address] = []\n                token_map[contract_address].append(token_data)\n        # Filter: only tokens present on at least two exchanges\n        common_tokens = {\n            k: v for k, v in token_map.items() if len(v) >= 2\n        }\n        return common_tokens\n\n    def find_arbitrage_opportunities(\n        self,\n        tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]],\n        threshold: float = 1.8,\n    ) -> List[ArbitrageOpportunity]:\n        """\n        Finds arbitrage opportunities for tokens available on at least two exchanges.\n        Returns a list of ArbitrageOpportunity objects where price difference > threshold (%).\n        """\n        opportunities: List[ArbitrageOpportunity] = []\n        common_tokens = self.get_common_tokens(tokens_by_dex)\n        for contract_address, token_datas in common_tokens.items():\n            # Find lowest and highest price and corresponding exchanges\n            lowest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            highest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            for td in token_datas:\n                if lowest is None or td.price < lowest.price:\n                    lowest = td\n                if highest is None or td.price > highest.price:\n                    highest = td\n            if lowest is None or highest is None or lowest.price == 0:\n                continue\n            price_diff_percent = ((highest.price - lowest.price) / lowest.price) * 100\n            if price_diff_percent > threshold:\n                opportunity = ArbitrageOpportunity(\n                    token_name=lowest.name,\n                    contract_address=contract_address,\n                    lowest_price=lowest.price,\n                    highest_price=highest.price,\n                    lowest_exchange=lowest.exchange,\n                    highest_exchange=highest.exchange,\n                    price_diff_percent=price_diff_percent,\n                )\n                opportunities.append(opportunity)\n        return opportunities'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of token_analyzer.py is complete, as the TokenAnalyzer, TokenData, and ArbitrageOpportunity classes have been written according to the system design. Calls fetch_and_analyze_callback to get arbitrage opportunities.\n        Updates every second.\n        """\n        st.set_page_config(page_title="Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard", layout="wide")\n        st.title("Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard")\n        st.caption("Monitoring Uniswap, PancakeSwap, and Sushiswap for arbitrage opportunities (refreshes every second)")\n\n        refresh_interval = 1  # seconds\n\n        # Streamlit\'s session state to persist data between reruns\n        if "last_update" not in st.session_state:\n            st.session_state["last_update"] = 0\n\n        # Main loop\n        while True:\n            start_time = time.time()\n            with st.spinner("Fetching latest token data and analyzing opportunities..."):\n                opportunities = fetch_and_analyze_callback()\n            st.subheader("Arbitrage Opportunities (>1.8% price difference)")\n            self.render_table(opportunities)\n            st.write(f"Last updated: {time.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\')}")\n            # Wait for the next refresh\n            elapsed = time.time() - start_time\n            if elapsed < refresh_interval:\n                time.sleep(refresh_interval - elapsed)\n            # Rerun Streamlit script\n            st.experimental_rerun()\n\n# Example usage (for main.py):\n# from token_analyzer import TokenAnalyzer\n# analyzer = TokenAnalyzer([...])\n# dashboard = Dashboard(analyzer)\n# dashboard.run(fetch_and_analyze_callback)'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of dashboard.py is complete, as the Dashboard class using Streamlit has been written. Async programming (asyncio, aiohttp) will be used for efficient real-time updates. Error handling and rate limiting will be built in.\\n004|\\n005|## File list\\n006|\\n007|- main.py\\n008|- dex_fetcher.py\\n009|- token_analyzer.py\\n010|- dashboard.py\\n011|- config.py\\n012|- requirements.txt\\n013|- docs/system_design.md\\n014|\\n015|## Data structures and interfaces:\\n016|\\n017|```mermaid\\n018|classDiagram\\n019|    class DEXFetcher {\\n020|        +__init__(name: str, endpoint: str)\\n021|        +fetch_tokens() -> dict[str, TokenData]\\n022|    }\\n023|    class TokenData {\\n024|        +name: str\\n025|        +contract_address: str\\n026|        +price: float\\n027|        +exchange: str\\n028|    }\\n029|    class TokenAnalyzer {\\n030|        +__init__(dex_fetchers: list[DEXFetcher])\\n031|        +get_common_tokens() -> dict[str, list[TokenData]]\\n032|        +find_arbitrage_opportunities(threshold: float=1.8) -> list[ArbitrageOpportunity]\\n033|    }\\n034|    class ArbitrageOpportunity {\\n035|        +token_name: str\\n036|        +contract_address: str\\n037|        +lowest_price: float\\n038|        +highest_price: float\\n039|        +lowest_exchange: str\\n040|        +highest_exchange: str\\n041|        +price_diff_percent: float\\n042|    }\\n043|    class Dashboard {\\n044|        +__init__(analyzer: TokenAnalyzer)\\n045|        +render_table(opportunities: list[ArbitrageOpportunity])\\n046|        +run()\\n047|    }\\n048|    DEXFetcher <.. def fetch_tokens(self) -> Dict[str, TokenData]:\n        """\n        Fetches token data from the DEX GraphQL endpoint.\n        Returns a dict mapping contract_address to TokenData.\n        """\n        # GraphQL queries for each DEX (Uniswap, PancakeSwap, Sushiswap)\n        # These queries fetch top tokens by liquidity and their price.\n        # For production, you may want to paginate or fetch more tokens.\n        query = """\n        {\n          tokens(first: 100, orderBy: volumeUSD, orderDirection: desc) {\n            id\n            symbol\n            name\n            derivedETH\n            derivedUSD\n          }\n        }\n        """\n        headers = {\n            "Content-Type": "application/json"\n        }\n        payload = {\n            "query": query\n        }\n        tokens = {}\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.post(self.endpoint, json=payload, headers=headers, timeout=15) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        print(f"[{self.name}] Error: Received status {resp.status}")\n                        return tokens\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    for token in data.get("data", {}).get("tokens", []):\n                        contract_address = token.get("id")\n                        name = token.get("name") or token.get("symbol") or contract_address\n                        # Prefer derivedUSD if available, else fallback to derivedETH (approximate with ETH price)\n                        price = None\n                        if token.get("derivedUSD") is not None:\n                            try:\n                                price = float(token["derivedUSD"])\n                            except Exception:\n                                price = None\n                        elif token.get("derivedETH") is not None:\n                            # Fallback: Use ETH price from        tokens[contract_address] = TokenData(\n                                name=name,\n                                contract_address=contract_address,\n                                price=price,\n                                exchange=self.name\n                            )\n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f"[{self.name}] Exception during fetch: {e}")\n        return tokens\n\n    async def _get_eth_price(self) -> float:\n        """\n        Fetches the current ETH price in USD from CoinGecko.\n        Used as a fallback if derivedUSD is not available.\n        """\n        url = "https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3/simple/price?ids=ethereum&vs_currencies=usd"\n        try:\n            async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\n                async with session.get(url, timeout=10) as resp:\n                    if resp.status != 200:\n                        return 0.0\n                    data = await resp.json()\n                    return Filter: only tokens present on at least two exchanges\n        common_tokens = {\n            k: v for k, v in token_map.items() if len(v) >= 2\n        }\n        return common_tokens\n\n    def find_arbitrage_opportunities(\n        self,\n        tokens_by_dex: List[Dict[str, TokenData]],\n        threshold: float = 1.8,\n    ) -> List[ArbitrageOpportunity]:\n        """\n        Finds arbitrage opportunities for tokens available on at least two exchanges.\n        Returns a list of ArbitrageOpportunity objects where price difference > threshold (%).\n        """\n        opportunities: List[ArbitrageOpportunity] = []\n        common_tokens = self.get_common_tokens(tokens_by_dex)\n        for contract_address, token_datas in common_tokens.items():\n            # Find lowest and highest price and corresponding exchanges\n            lowest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            highest: Optional[TokenData] = None\n            for td in token_datas:\n                if lowest is None or td.price < lowest.price:\n                    lowest = td\n                if highest is None or td.price > highest.price:\n                    highest = td\n            if lowest is None or highest is None or lowest.price == 0:\n                continue\n            price_diff_percent = ((highest.price - lowest.price) / lowest.price) * 100\n            if price_diff_percent > threshold:\n                opportunity = ArbitrageOpportunity(\n                    token_name=lowest.name,\n                    contract_address=contract_address,\n                    lowest_price=lowest.price,\n                    highest_price=highest.price,\n                    lowest_exchange=lowest.exchange,\n                    highest_exchange=highest.exchange,\n                    price_diff_percent=price_diff_percent,\n                )\n                opportunities.append(opportunity)\n        return opportunities'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of token_analyzer.py is complete, as the TokenAnalyzer, TokenData, and ArbitrageOpportunity classes have been written according to the system design. Calls fetch_and_analyze_callback to get arbitrage opportunities.\n        Updates every second.\n        """\n        st.set_page_config(page_title="Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard", layout="wide")\n        st.title("Real-Time DEX Arbitrage Dashboard")\n        st.caption("Monitoring Uniswap, PancakeSwap, and Sushiswap for arbitrage opportunities (refreshes every second)")\n\n        refresh_interval = 1  # seconds\n\n        # Streamlit\'s session state to persist data between reruns\n        if "last_update" not in st.session_state:\n            st.session_state["last_update"] = 0\n\n        # Main loop\n        while True:\n            start_time = time.time()\n            with st.spinner("Fetching latest token data and analyzing opportunities..."):\n                opportunities = fetch_and_analyze_callback()\n            st.subheader("Arbitrage Opportunities (>1.8% price difference)")\n            self.render_table(opportunities)\n            st.write(f"Last updated: {time.strftime(\'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\')}")\n            # Wait for the next refresh\n            elapsed = time.time() - start_time\n            if elapsed < refresh_interval:\n                time.sleep(refresh_interval - elapsed)\n            # Rerun Streamlit script\n            st.experimental_rerun()\n\n# Example usage (for main.py):\n# from token_analyzer import TokenAnalyzer\n# analyzer = TokenAnalyzer([...])\n# dashboard = Dashboard(analyzer)\n# dashboard.run(fetch_and_analyze_callback)'}, {'role': 'assistant', 'content': 'The implementation of dashboard.py is complete, as the Dashboard class using Streamlit has been written.
